And he baruk Yahuah who had led him out of Ur of the Chaldees, and had brought him to this land.

In the first year, in the seventh week, on the new moon of the first month, he built an mizbe'ach on this mountain, and called on the name of Yahuah and said, "You, the eternal Aluahim, are my Aluahim."
He offered on the mizbe'ach a burnt sacrifice to Yahuah that He should be with him and not forsake him all the days of his life.

He left that place and went toward the south, and he came to Hebron and Hebron was built at that time, and he lived there two years, and he went from that place into the land of the south, to Bealoth, and there was a famine in the land. llAbram went into Mitsrayim in the third year of the week, and he dwelt in Mitsrayim five years before his wife was torn away from him.
Now, Tanais in Mitsrayim was built seven years after Hebron.
When Pharaoh seized Sarai, the wife of Aḇram Yahuah plagued Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Aḇram's wife.

Aḇram was celebrated and admired because of his great possessions of sheep, and cattle, and donkeys, and horses, and camels, and menservants, and maidservants, and in silver and gold. Lot and his brother's son were also wealthy.

Pharaoh gave back Sarai, the wife of Aḇram, and he sent him out of the land of Mitsrayim, and he journeyed to the place where he had pitched his tent at the beginning, to the place of the mizbe'ach, with Ai on the east, and Bethel on the west, and he baruk Yahuah his Aluahim who had brought him back in peace.

In the forty-first jubilee in the third year of the first week, that he returned to this place and offered on it a burnt sacrifice, and called on the name of Yahuah, and said, "You, the most high Aluahim, are my Aluahim forever and ever."
In the fourth year of this week Lot parted from him, and Lot lived in Sodom, and the men of Sodom sinned greatly.
It grieved him in his heart that his brother's son had parted from him because Aḇram had no children.

After Lot had parted from him, in the fourth year of this week. In that year when Lot was taken captive, Yahuah said to Aḇram, "Lift up your eyes from the place where you are dwelling, northward and southward, and westward and eastward.

All the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever, and I will make your offs pring as the sand of the sea, though a man may number the dust of the earth, yet your offspring shall not be numbered.
Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and the breadth of it, and see it all. To your offspring will I give it." And Aḇram went to Hebron, and lived there.

And in this year came Chedorlaomer, king of Elam, and Amraphel, king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Sellasar, and Tergal, king of nations, and killed the king of Gomorrah , and the king of Sodom fled, and many fell through wounds in the valley of Sid dim, by the Salt Sea.
They took captive Sodom and Adawm and Zeboim, and they took Lot captive, the son of Aḇram's brother, and all his possessions, and they went to Dan.
One who had escaped came and told Aḇram that his brother's son had been taken captive.

And Aḇram equipped his household servants for Aḇram, and for his offspring, a tenth of the first-fruits to Yahuah, and Yahuah ordained it as a law forever that they should give it to the kohanim who served before Him, that they should possess it forever.

There is no limit of days to this law, for He has ordained it for the generations forever that they should give to Yahuah the tenth of everything, of the seed and of the wine and of the oil and of the cattle and of the sheep.
He gave it to His kohanim to eat and to drink with joy before Him.
The king of Sodom came and bowed down to him, and said, "Our Yahuah Aḇram, give to us the nepheshoth which you have rescued, but let the booty be yours."

And Aḇram said to him, "I lift up my hands to the Most High Aluahim, that from a thread to a shoe-latchet I shall not take anything that is yours so that you could never say, I have made Aḇram rich, except only what the young men, Aner and Eschol, and Mamre have eaten, and the portion of the men who went with me. These shall take their portion." [

After these things, in the fourth year of this week, on the new moon of the third month, the word of Yahuah came to Aḇram in a dream, saying, "Fear not, Aḇram, I am your defender, and your reward will be very great."

He said, "Yahuah, Yahuah, what will you give me, seeing I go from here childless, and the son of Maseq, the son of my handmaid, Eliezer of Damascus, he will be my heir, and to me you have given no offspring."
He said to him, "This man will not be your heir, but one that will come out of your own bowels. He will be your heir."
And He brought him out abroad, and said to him, "Look toward shamayim and number the stars if you are able to number them."
He looked toward shamayim, and beheld the stars. And He said to him, "so shall your offspring be."
And he believed in Yahuah, and it was counted to him as righteousness.

Aluahim said to him, "1 am Yahuah that brought you out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give you the land of the Canaanites to possess it forever, and I will be Aluahim to you and to your offspring after you."
He said, "Yahuah, Yahuah, how shall I know that I shall inherit it?"
Aluahim said to him, "Take Me a heifer of three years, and a goat of three years, and a sheep of three years, and a turtle-dove, and a pigeon."
And he took all these in the middle of the month and he dwelt at the oak of Mamre, which is near Hebron.
He built an mizbe'ach there, and sacrificed all these. He poured their blood on the mizbe'ach, and divided them in half, and laid them over against each other, but the birds he did not divide.
Birds came down on the pieces, and Aḇram drove them away, and did not permit the birds to touch them.

It happened, when the sun had set, that an ecstasy fell on Aḇram, and such a horror of great darkness fell on him, and it was said to Aḇram, "Know of a surety that your offspring shall be a stranger in a land that is not theirs, and they shall be brought into bondage, and afflicted for four hundred years.
The nation also to whom they will be in bondage will I judge, and after that they shall come out from that place with many possessions.
You will go to your fathers in peace, and be buried in a good old age.
But in the fourth generation they shall return here, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet full."
And he awoke from his sleep, and he arose, and the sun had set; and there was a flame, and a furnace was smoking, and a flame of fire passed between the pieces.

On that day Yahuah made a berith with Aḇram, saying, "To your offspring will I give this land, from the river of Mitsrayim to the great river, the river Euphrates, the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites, the Perizzites, and the Rephaim, the Phakorites, and the Hivites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
The day passed, and Aḇram offered the pieces, and the birds, and their fruit offerings, and their drink offerings, and the fire devoured them.

On that day we made a berith with Aḇram, in the same way we had covenanted with Noaḥ in this month; and Aḇram renewed the festival and laws for himself forever. 21Abram rejoiced, and made all these things known to Sarai his wife. He believed that he would have offsp ring, but she did not bear.

Sarai advised her husband Aḇram, and said to him, "Go in to Haḡar, my Mitsrite maid, it may be that I shall build up offspring to you by her." 23Abram listened to the voice of Sarai his wife, and said to her, "Do so." And Sarai took Haḡar, her maid, the Mitsrite, and gave her to Aḇram, her husband, to be his wife.
He went in to her, and she conceived and gave birth to a son, and he called his name Yishma'al, in the fifth year of this week; and this was the eighty-sixth year in the life of Aḇram. [
In the fifth year of the fourth week of this jubilee, in the third month, in the middle of the month, Aḇram celebrated the feas t of the first-fruits of the grain harvest.

And he made new offerings on the mizbe'ach, the first-fruits of the produce to Yahuah, a heifer, and a goat, and a sheep on the mizbe'ach as a burnt sacrifice to Yahuah; their fruit offerings and their drink offerings he offered on the mizbe'ach with frankincense.
Yahuah appeared to Aḇram, and said to him, "I am Aluahim Almighty. Examine yourself and demonstrate yourself before me and be perfect.
I will make My berith between Me and you, and I will multiply you greatly."
Aḇram fell on his face, and Aluahim talked with him, and said, "My law is with you, and you will be the father of many nations.
Neither shall your name any more be called Aḇram, but your name from now on, even forever, shall be Aḇraham.
For I have made you the father of many nations.
I will make you very great, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come forth from you.
I shall establish My berith between Me and you, and your offspring after you, throughout their generations, for an eternal berith, so that I may be a Aluahim to you, and to your offspring after you.
You may possess the land where you have been a sojourner, the land of Kena'an, and you will possess it forever, and I will be their Aluahim."

Yahuah said to Aḇraham, "Keep my berith, you and your offspring after you, and circumcise every male among you, and circumcise your foreskins, and it shall be a token of an eternal berith between Me and you.

And the eighth day you shall circumcise the child, every male throughout your generations, him that is born in the house, or whom you have bought with money from any stranger, whom you have acquired who is not of your offspring.
He that is born in your house shall surely be circumcised, and those whom you have bought with money shall be circumcised, and My berith shall be in your flesh for an eternal ordinance.
The uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin on the eighth day, that nephesh shall be cut off from his people, for he has broken My berith."
Aluahim said to Aḇraham, " As for Sarai your wife, her name shall no more be called Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
I will bless her, and give you a son by her, and I will bless him, and he shall become a nation, and kings of nations shall proceed from him."
Aḇraham fell on his face, and rejoiced, and said in his heart, "Shall a son be born to him that is a hundred years old, and shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bring forth?"
Aḇraham said to Aluahim, "Oh, that Yishma'al might live before you!"
Aluahim said, "Yea, and Sarah also shall bear you a son, and you will call his name Yitsḥaq, and I will establish My berith with him, an everlasting berith, and for his offspring after him.
And as for Yishma'al also have I heard you, and behold I will bless him, and make him great, and multiply him greatly, and he shall beget twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation.
But My berith will I establish with Yitsḥaq, whom Sarah shall bear to you this time next year."
Aluahim ceased speaking with him, and Aluahim went up from Aḇraham.

Aḇraham did according as Aluahim had said to him, and he took Yishma'al his son, and all that were born in his house, and whom he had bought with his money, every male in his house, and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin.
On that same day was Aḇraham circumcised, and all the men of his house, and all those whom he had bought with money from the children of the stranger were circumcised with him.

This law is for all the generations forever, and there is no variance of days, and no omission of one day out of the eight days, for it is an eternal law, ordained and written on the heavenly tablets.

Every one that is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the eighth day, does not belong to the children of the berith which Yahuah made with Aḇraham, but instead they belong to the children of destruction; nor is there any other sign on him that he is Yahuah's, but he is destined to be destroyed and killed from the earth, and to be rooted out of the earth, for he has broken the berith of Yahuah our Aluahim.

All the mal'akim of the presence (of Yahuah) and all the mal'akim of sanctification have been created already circumcised from the day of their creation, and before the mal'akim of the presence (of Yahuah) and the mal'akim of sanctification He has sanctified Yasharal, that they should be with Him and with His holy mal'akim.
Command the children of Yasharal and let them observe the sign of this berith for their generations as an eternal law, and they will not be rooted out of the land.
For the command is ordained for a berith, that they should observe it forever among all the children of Yasharal.

For Yishma'al and his sons and his brothers, and Ĕsaw, Yahuah did not cause them to come to Him, and he did not choose them. Although they are the children of Aḇraham, He knew them, but He chose Yasharal to be His people.

He sanctified them, and gathered them from among all the children of men; for there are many nations and many peoples , and all are His, and over all nations He has placed ruchot in authority to lead them astray from Him.

But over Yasharal He did not appoint any mal'ak or spirit, for He alone is their ruler, and He will preserve them and require them at the hand of His mal'akim and His ruchot, and at the hand of all His powers in order that He may preserve them and bless them, that they may be His and He may be theirs from now on forever.

I announce to you that the children of Yasharal will not keep true to this law, and they will not circumcise their sons according to all this law; for in the flesh of their circumcision they will omit this circumcision of their sons, and all of the sons of Beliar will leave their sons uncircumcised as they were born.

There will be great wrath from Yahuah against the children of Yasharal because they have forsaken His berith and turned aside from His word, and provoked (Aluahim) and blasphemed, because they do not observe the ordinance of this law; for they have treated their genitalia like the Gentiles, so that they may be removed and rooted out of the land. And there will no more be pardon or forgiveness to them for all the sin of this eternal error. [
On the new moon of the fourth month we appeared to Aḇraham, at the oak of Mamre, and we talked with him, and we announced to him that Sarah, his wife, would give him a son.
And Sarah laughed, for she heard that we had spoken these words to Aḇraham. We warned her, and she became afraid, and denied that she had laughed because of the words.
We told her the name of her son, as his name is ordained and written in the heavenly tablets and it is Yitsḥaq.
We told her that when we returned to her at a set time, she would have conceived a son.

In this month Yahuah executed his mishpatim on Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Zeboim, and all the region of the Yarden, and He burned them with fire and brimstone, and destroyed them and they are destroyed until this day, because of all their works. They are wicked and vast sinners, and they defile themselves and commit fornication in their flesh, and work uncleanness on the earth as I have told you.
In like manner, Aluahim will execute mishpat on the places where they have done similar to the uncleanness of the Sodomites, and they will suffer a mishpat like that of Sad am.
But for Lot, we made an exception, for Aluahim remembered Aḇraham, and sent him out from the place of the overthrow.
And he and his daughters committed sin on the earth, such as had not been on the earth since the days of Adawm until his time, for the man had sex with his daughters.

It was commanded and engraved concerning all his offsp ring, on the heavenly tablets, to remove them and root them out, and to execute mishpat on them like the mishpat of Sodom, and to leave no offspring of that man on earth on the day of condemnation.
In this month Aḇraham moved from Hebron, and departed and lived between Kadesh and Shur in the mountains of Gerar.
In the middle of the fifth month he moved from that place, and lived at the Well of the Oath.
In the middle of the sixth month Yahuah visited Sarah and did to her as He had spoken and she conceived.
And she gave birth to a son in the third month. In the middle of the month, at the time of which Yahuah had spoken to Aḇraham, on the festival of the first-fruits of the harvest, Yitsḥaq was born.
Aḇraham circumcised his son on the eighth day, he was the first that was circumcised according to the berith that is ordained forever.
In the sixth year of the fourth week we came to Aḇraham at the Well of the Oath, and we appeared to him.

We returned in the seventh month, and found Sarah with child before us and we baruk him, and we announced to him all the things that had been decreed concerning him, so that he should not die until he should beget six more sons and saw them before he died.

But in Yitsḥaq should his name and offspring be called, and that all the offspring of his sons should be Gentiles, and be counted with the Gentiles; but from the sons of Yitsḥaq one should become a holy offspring, and should not be counted among the Gentiles.

For he should become the portion (dowry) of the Most High, and all his offspring had fallen into the possession of Aluahim, that they should be to Yahuah a people for His possession above all nations and that they should become a kingdom and kohanim and a holy nation.
We went our way, and we announced to Sarah all that we had told him, and they both rejoiced with very great joy.

He built there an mizbe'ach to Yahuah who had delivered him, and who was causing him to rejoice in the land of his sojourning, and he celebrated a festival of joy in this month for seven days, near the mizbe'ach which he had built at the Well of the Oath.
He built tents for himself and for his servants on this festival, and he was the first to celebrate the feast of Sukkot on the earth.

During these seven days he brought a burnt offering to Yahuah each day to the mizbe'ach consisting of two oxen, two rams, seven sheep, one male goat, for a sin offering that he might atone thereby for himself and for his offspring.

As an offering of thanks he brought, seven rams, seven kids, seven sheep, and seven male goats, and their fruit offerings and their drink offerings; and he burnt all the fat of it on the mizbe'ach, a chosen offering to Yahuah for a sweet smelling odor.

Morning and evening he burnt fragran t substances, frankincense and incense, and sweet spice, and nard, and myrrh, and spice, and aromatic plants; all these seven he offered, crushed, mixed together in equal parts and pure.

And he celebrated this feast during seven days, rejoicing with all his heart and with all his nephesh, he and all those who were in his house, and there was no stranger with him, nor any that was uncircumcised.

He baruk his Creator who had created him in his generation, for He had created him according to His good pleasure. Aluahim knew and perceived that from him would arise the plant of righteousness for the eternal generations, and from him a holy offspring, so that it should become like Him who had made all things.
He baruk and rejoiced, and he called the name of this festival the festival of Yahuah, a joy acceptable to the Most High Aluahim.

And we baruk him forever, and all his offspring after him throughout all the gene rations of the earth, because he celebrated this festival in its season, according to the eduth of the heavenly tablets.

For this reason it is ordained on the heavenly tablets concerning Yasharal, that they shall celebrate the feast of Sukkot seven days with joy, in the seventh month, accep table before Yahuah as a statute forever throughout their generations every year.

To this there is no limit of days; for it is ordained forever regarding Yasharal that they should celebrate it and dwell in tents, and set wreaths on their heads, and take leafy boughs, and willows from the brook. 31Abraham took branches of palm trees, and the fruit of good and pleasing trees, and every day going round the mizbe'ach with the branches seven times a day in the morning, he praised and gave thanks to his Aluahim for all things in joy. [
In the first year of the fifth week Yitsḥaq was weaned in this jubilee, and Aḇraham made a great banquet in the third month, on the day his son Yitsḥaq was wean ed.
Yishma'al, the son of Haḡar, the Mitsrite, was in front of Aḇraham, his father, in his place, and Aḇraham rejoiced and baruk Aluahim because he had seen his sons and had not died childless.

He remembered the words which He had spoken to him on the day that Lot had departed from him, and he rejoiced because Yahuah had given him offspring on the earth to inherit the earth, and he baruk with all his mouth the Creator of all things.

Sarah saw Yishma'al playing and dancing, and Aḇraham rejoicing with great joy, and she became jealous of Yishma'al and said to Aḇraham, "Throw out this bondwoman and her son. The son of this bondwoman will not be heir with my son, Yitsḥaq."
And the situation was troubling to Aḇraham, because of his maidservant and because of his son, because he did not want to drive them from him.

Aluahim said to Aḇraham "Let it not be troubling in your sight, because of the child and because of the bondwoman. Listen to Sarah and to all her words and do them, for in Yitsḥaq shall your name and offspring be called.
But as for the son of this bondwoman I will make him a great nation, because he is of your offspring."
Aḇraham got up early in the morning, and took bread and a bottle of water, and placed them on the shoulders of Haḡar and the child, and sent her away.
And she departed and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba, and the water in the bottle was spent, and the child was thirsty, and was not able to go on, and fell down.

His mother took him and laid him under an olive tree, and went and sat her down over away from him at the distance of a bow-shot; for she said, "Let me not see the death of my child," and she sat and wept.
An mal'ak of Aluahim, one of the holy ones, said to her, "Why do you weep, Haḡar? Stand. Take the child, and hold him in your hand, for Aluahim has heard your voice, and has seen the child."
She opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water, and she went and filled her bottle with water, and she gave her child a drin k, and she arose and went towards the wilderness of Paran.
And the child grew and became an archer, and Aluahim was with him, and his mother took him a wife from among the daughters of Mitsrayim.
She (the wife) gave birth to a son, and he called his name Nebaioth; for she said, "Yahuah was close to me when I called on him."

In the seventh week, in the first year of it, in the first mon th in this jubilee, on the twelfth of this month, there were voices in shamayim regarding Aḇraham, that he was faithful in all that He told him, and that he loved Yahuah, and that in every affliction he was faithful.

Prince Mastema came and said before Aluahim, "Look, Aḇraham loves Yitsḥaq his son, and he delights in him above all things, tell him to offer him as a burnt-offering on the mizbe'ach, and You will see if he will do this command, and You will know if he is faithful in everyway that You test him.

Yahuah knew that Aḇraham was faithful throughout all his afflictions, for He had tried him through his country and with famine, and had tried him with the wealth of kings, and had tried him again through his wife, when she was torn from him, and with circumcision ; and had tried him through Yishma'al and Haḡar, his maid-servant, when he sent them away.
In everything that He had tried him, he was found faithful, and his nephesh was not impatient, and he was not slow to act, because he was faithful and a lover of Yahuah. [
Aluahim said to him, " Aḇraham. Aḇraham." and he said, "Look, here am I."
He said, "Take your beloved son, Yitsḥaq, whom you love, and go to the high country, and offer him on one of the moun tains which I will point out to you."

He got early in the morning and saddled his donkey, and took two young men with him, and Yitsḥaq his son, and split the wood of the burnt offering, and he went to the place on the third day, and he saw the place afar off.

He came to a well of water (near Mount Moriah), and he said to his young men, "You stay here with the donkey, and I and the lad shall go yonder, and when we have worshipped we shall come back to you."
He took the wood of the burnt-offering and laid it on Yitsḥaq his son, and he took the fire and the knife, and they went both of them together to that place.
Yitsḥaq said to his father, "Father" and he said, "Here am I, my son." He said to him, "Look, we have the fire, and the knife, and the wood, but where is the sheep for the burnt -offering, father?"
He said, "Aluahim will provide for himself a sheep for a burn t-offering, my son." And he neared the place of the mountain of Aluahim.

He built an mizbe'ach, and he placed the wood on the mizbe'ach, and bound Yitsḥaq his son, and placed him on the wood that was on the mizbe'ach, and stretched out his hand to take the knife to kill Yitsḥaq, his son.

I stood in the presence of him, and before prince Mastema, (and the holy mal'akim stood and wept over the mizbe'ach as prince Mastema and his mal'akim rejoiced and said "Yitsḥaq will be destroyed and we will see if Aḇraham is faithful), and Yahuah said, "Command him not to lay his hand on the lad, nor to do anything to him, for I have shown that he fears Yahuah."
I called to him from shamayim, and said to him, "Aḇraham, Aḇraham." and he was terrified and said, "Here am I."
I said to him, "Lay not your hand on the lad, neither do anything to him; for now I have shown that you fear Yahuah, and have not withheld your son, your first-born son, from me."

Prince Mastema was put to shame (and was bound by the mal'akim); and Aḇraham lifted up his eyes and looked and saw a ram caught by his horns, and Aḇraham went and took the ram and offered it as a burnt-offering in place of his son.
Aḇraham called that place "Yahuah has seen,"
Yahuah called Aḇraham by his name a second time from shamayim, as he caused us to appear to speak to him in the name of Yahuah.

He said, "By Myself have I sworn," said Yahuah, "Because you have done this thing, and have not withheld your son, your beloved son, from Me, that in berakah I will bless you, and in multiplying I will multiply your offspring as the stars of shamayim, and as the sand which is on the seashore.

Your offspring shall inherit the cities of their enemies, and in your offspring shall all nations of the earth be baruk. Because you have obeyed My voice, and I have shown to all that you are faithful to Me in all that I have said to you, "Go in peace."
Aḇraham went back to his young men, and they stood and went back together to Beersheba, and Aḇraham lived by the Well of the Oath.
And he celebrated this festival every year, seven days with joy, and he called it the festival of Yahuah according to the seven days during which he went and returned in peace.
Accordingly, it has been ordained and written on the heavenly tablets regarding Yasharal and its children that they should observe this festival seven days with the joy of festival. [
In the first year of the first week in the forty-second jubilee, Aḇraham returned and lived across from Hebron, in Kirjath Arba for two weeks of years.
In the first year of the third week of this jubilee the days of the life of Sarah were completed, and she died in Hebron.

Aḇraham went to mourn over her and bury her, and we tested him to see if his spirit was patient and he had neither anger nor contempt in the words of his mouth, and he was found patient in this and was not disturbed.
In patience of spirit he discussed with the children of Heth that they should give him a place in which to bury his dead.

And Yahuah gave him grace before all who saw him, and he asked the sons of Heth in gentleness, and they gave him the land of the double cave over beside Mamre, that is Hebron, for four hundred pieces of silver.

They said to him, "We shall give it to you for nothing," but he would not take it from them for nothing, for he gave the price of the place and paid the money in full. And he bowed down before them twice, and after this he buried his dead in the double cave.
All the days of the life of Sarah were one hundred and twenty-seven years, that is, two jubilees and four weeks and one year, these are the days of the years of the life of Sarah.
This is the tenth trial with which Aḇraham was tested, and he was found faithful and patient in spirit.

He did not say a single word regarding the rumor in the land of how Aluahim had said that He would give it to him and to his offs pring after him, but instead he begged for a place there to bury his dead. Because he was found faithful, it was recorded on the heavenly tablets that he was the friend of Aluahim.

In the fourth year of it (this jubilee) he took a wife for his son Yitsḥaq and her name was Riḇqah the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Nahor, the brother of Aḇraham the sister of Laban and daughter of Bethuel; and Bethuel was the son of Melca, who was the wife of Nahor, the brother of Aḇraham.

Aḇraham took to himself a third wife from among the daughters of his household servants, for Haḡar had died before Sarah, and her name was Keturah,. And she gave birth to six sons, Zimram, and Jokshan, and Medan, and Miḏyan, and Ishbak, and Shuah, in the two weeks of years.
In the sixth week, in the second year of it, Riḇqah gave birth to two sons of Yitsḥaq, Ya'aqoḇ and Ĕsaw.
And Ya'aqoḇ had no beard and was a straight and tall man who dwelt in tents, and Ĕsaw was a powerful a man of the field, and was hairy.

The youths grew, and Ya'aqoḇ learned to write, but Ĕsaw did not learn, for he was a man of the field and a hunter, and he learned war, and all his deeds were fierce. 15Abraham loved Ya'aqoḇ, but Yitsḥaq loved Ĕsaw.

And Aḇraham saw the deeds of Ĕsaw, and he knew that in Ya'aqoḇ should his name and offspring be called. He called Riḇqah and gave mitsvah regarding Ya'aqoḇ, for he knew that she too loved Ya'aqoḇ much more than Ĕsaw.
He said to her, "My daughter, watch over my son Ya'aqoḇ, for he shall take my place on the earth. He shall be a berakah throughout the children of men and for the glory of all the offspring of Shem.
I know that Yahuah will choose him to be a people (nation) and a possession to Himself, above all peoples that are on the face of the earth.
Yitsḥaq, my son, loves Ĕsaw more than Ya'aqoḇ, but I see that you truly love Ya'aqoḇ.
Add still further to your kindness to him, and regard him in love, for he shall be a berakah to us on the earth from now on to all generations of the earth.
Let your hands be strong and let your heart rejoice in your son Ya'aqoḇ, for I have loved him far beyond all my sons. He shall be baruk forever, and his offspring shall fill the whole earth.
If a man can number the sand of the earth, his offspring also shall be numbered.
And all the berakoth with which Yahuah has baruk me and my offspring shall belong to Ya'aqoḇ and his offspring always.

In his offspring shall my name be baruk, and the name of my fathers, Shem, Noaḥ, Ḥanoḵ, Mahalalel, Enos, Sheth, and Adawm. And these shall serve to lay the foundations of the shamayim, and to strengthen the earth, and to renew all the stars and planets which are in the sky.

He called Ya'aqoḇ and kissed him in front of Riḇqah, his mother, and baruk him, and said, "Ya'aqoḇ, my beloved son, whom my nephesh loves, may Aluahim bless you from above the sky, and may He give you all the berakoth with which He baruk Adawm, Ḥanoḵ, Noaḥ, and Shem; and all the things of which He told me, and all the things which He promised to give me, may He cause to be yours and your offspring forever, accord ing to the days of shamayim above the earth.
And the Ruchot of Mastema shall not rule over you or over your offspring or turn you from Yahuah, who is your Aluahim from now on forever.
May Yahuah Aluahim be a father to you and may you be like His first-born son, and to the people always. Go in peace, my son."
And they both went out together from Aḇraham.
Riḇqah loved Ya'aqoḇ, with all her heart and with all her nephesh, very much more than Ĕsaw, but Yitsḥaq loved Ĕsaw much more than Ya'aqoḇ. [
In the forty-second jubilee, in the first year of the seventh week, Aḇraham called Yishma'al, and his twelve sons, and Yitsḥaq and his two sons, and the six sons of Keturah, and their sons.

And he commanded them that they should observe the way of Yahuah, that they should work righteousness, and love each his neighbor, and act in this manner among all men, that they should each walk with regard to the ways of Yahuah to do mishpat and righteousness on the earth.

He also commanded them that they should circumcise their sons, according to the berith, which Aluahim had made with them, and not deviate to the right or the left of all the paths which Yahuah had commanded us, and that we should keep ourselves from all fornication and un cleanness.

He said, "If any woman or maid commits fornication among you, burn her with fire. And do not let them commit fornication with her with their eyes or their heart; and do not let them take to themselves wives from the daughters of Kena'an, because the offspring of Kena'an will be rooted out of the land."

He told them about the mishpat on the giants, and the mishpat on the Sodom ites, how they had been ju dged because of their wickedness, and had died because of their fornication and uncleanness, and corruption through fornication together.

He said, "Guard yourselves from all fornication and unc leanness, and from all pollu tion of sin, or you will make our name a curse, and your whole life a shame, and all your sons to be destroyed by the sword, and you will become accursed like Sod om, and all that is left of you shall be as the sons of Gomorrah.
I implore you, my sons, love the Aluahim of shamayim and cling to all His mitsvot.

Do not walk after their idols and after their ways of uncleanness, and do not make yoursel ves molten or graven Aluahims. They are empty, and there is no spirit in them, for they are work of men's hands, and all who trust in them, trust in nothing.

Do not serve them, nor worship them, but serve the most high Aluahim, and worship Him continually, and hope for His presence always, and work uprightness and righteousness before Him, that He may have pleasure in you and grant you His mercy, and send rain on you morning and evening, and bless all your works which you have performed on the earth, and bless your bread and your water, and bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your land, and the herds of your cattle, and the flocks of your sheep.
You will be for a berakah on the earth, and all nations of the earth will desire you, and bless your sons in my name, that they may be baruk as I am."
He gave to Yishma'al and to his sons, and to the sons of Keturah, gifts, and sent them away from Yitsḥaq his son, and he gave everything to Yitsḥaq his son.
Yishma'al and his sons, and the sons of Keturah and their sons, went together and settled from Paran to the border of Babylon in all the land that is toward the East facing the desert.
These mingled (intermarried) with each other, and their names were called Arabs, and Ishmaelites. [
In the sixth year of the seventh week of this jubilee Aḇraham called Yitsḥaq his son, and commanded him, saying, "I have become old. I do not know the day of my death but I am full of my days.
I am one hundred and seventy-five years old, and throughout all the days of my life I have remembered Yahuah, and sought with all my heart to do His will, and to walk uprightly in all His ways.
My nephesh has hated idols. I have given my heart and spirit to the observance of the will of Him who created me.

For He is the living Aluahim, and He is holy and faithful, and He is righteous beyond all, and He is no respecter of men or of their gifts, for Aluahim is righteous, and executes ju dgment on all those who transgress His mitsvot and despise His berith. S My son, observe His mitsvot and His law and His mishpatim, and do not walk after the abominations and after the graven images and after the molten images.
And eat no blood at all of animals or cattle, or of any bird that flies in the shamayim.

If you kill a sacrificial animal as an acceptable peace offering, kill it, and pour out its blood on the mizbe'ach. Place all the fat of the offering on the mizbe'ach with fine flour and the meat offering mingled with oil with its drink offering. Place them all together on the mizbe'ach of burnt offering. It is a sweet odor before Yahuah.

You will offer the fat of the sacrifice of thanks offerings on the fire which is on the mizbe'ach, and the fat which is on the belly, and all the fat on the inside, behind the two kidneys, and all the fat that is on them, and lobes of the liver you will remove, together with the kidneys.
Offer all these for a sweet odor acceptable before Yahuah, with its meat­ offering and with its drink- offering, and the bread of the offering to Yahuah.

Eat its meat on that day and on the second day, but do not let the sun go down on it until it is eaten. Let nothing be left over for the third day, for it is not acceptable. Let it no longer be eaten, and all who eat of it will bring sin on themselves, for thus I have found it written in the books of my forefathers, and in the words of Ḥanoḵ, and in the words of Noaḥ.
On all your offerings you will scatter salt, and do not let the salt of the berith be lacking in all your offerings before Yahuah.

As regards the wood of the sacrifices, beware to bring only these and no other wood to the mizbe'ach in add ition to these, cypress, bay, almond, fir, pine, cedar, savin, fig, olive, myrrh, laurel, and aspalathus.

Of these kinds of wood lay on the mizbe'ach under the sacrifice, such as have been tested as to their appearance, and do not lay on it any split or dark wood, but only hard and clean wood, without fault, a heal thy, new growth. Do not lay old wood on it, because there is no longer fragrance in it as before.
Besides these kinds of wood there is none other that you will place on the mizbe'ach, for the fragrance is dispersed, and the smell of its fragrance will not go up to shamayim.
Observe this mitsvah and do it, my son, that you may be upright in all your deeds.

Be clean in your body at all times. Wash yourself with water before you approach to offer on the mizbe'ach. Wash your hands and your feet before you draw near to the mizbe'ach, and when you are done sacrificing, wash your hands and feet again.
Let no blood appear on you or on your clothes. Be on your guard against blood, my son. Be on your guard continually and cover it with dust.
Do not eat any blood for it is the nephesh. Eat no blood whatsoever.

Take no payment for shedding the blood of man, or it will cause it to be shed without fear of punishment, without mishpat. It is the blood that is shed that causes the earth to sin, and the earth cannot be cleansed from the blood of man except by the blood of he who shed it.

Take no present or gift for the blood of man, blood for blood, that you may be accepted before Yahuah, the Most High Aluahim. He is the defense of the good, so that you may be preserved from all evil, and that He may withhold you from every kind of death.
I see, my son, all the works of the children of men are sin and wickedness, and all their deeds are uncleanness and an abomination and a pollu tion, and there is no righteousness in them.

Beware, or you will walk in their ways and tread in their paths, and commit a sin worthy of death before the Most High Aluahim. He will hide His face from you and give you back into the hands of your transgression, and root you out of the land, and your offspring likewise from under shamayim, and your name and your offspring shall perish from the whole earth.
Turn away from all their deeds and all their uncleanness, and observe the laws of the Most High Aluahim, and do His will and be upright in all things.

If you do this, He will bless you in all your deeds, and will raise up from you a plant of righteousness through all the earth, throughout all gen erations of the earth, and my name and your name shall not be forgotten under shamayim forever.

Go, my son in peace. May the Most High Aluahim, my Aluahim and your Aluahim, strengthen you to do His will, and may He bless all your offspring and the remainder of your off spring for the generations forever, with all righteous berakoth, that you may be a berakah on all the earth."
And he went out from him rejoicing. [

In the first week in the forty-fourth jubilee, in the second year, that is, the year in which Aḇraham died, Yitsḥaq and Yishma'al came from the Well of the Oath to celebrate the feast of weeks which is the feast of the first-fruits of the harvest to Aḇraham, their father, and Aḇraham rejoiced because his two sons had come.
Yitsḥaq had many possessions in Beersheba, and Yitsḥaq desired to go and see his possessions and to return to his father.

In those days Yishma'al came to see his father, and they both came together, and Yitsḥaq offered a sacrifice for a burnt offering, and presented it on the mizbe'ach of his father that he had made in Hebron.

He offered a thanks offering and made a feast of joy in the presence of Yishma'al, his brother, and Riḇqah made new cakes from the new grain, and gave them to Ya'aqoḇ, her son, to take them to Aḇraham, his father, from the first-fruits of the land, that he might eat and bless the Creator of all things before he died.
Yitsḥaq, also, sent Ya'aqoḇ to Aḇraham with an offering of his best for thanks so that he might eat and drink.

He ate and drank, and baruk the Most High Aluahim, who has created shamayim and earth, who has made all the fat things of the earth, and given them to the children of men that they might eat and drink and bless their Creator.

" And now I give thanks to You, my Aluahim, because you have caused me to see this day, behold, I am one hundred three score and fifteen years, an old man and full of days, and all my days have been peace to me.
The sword of the adversary has not overcome me in all that You have given me and my children all the days of my life until this day.

My Aluahim, may Your mercy and Your peace be on Your servant, and on the offspring of his sons, that they may be to You a chosen nation and an inheritance from among all the nations of the earth from now on to all the days of the gene rations of the earth, to all the ages."

He called Ya'aqoḇ and said, "My son Ya'aqoḇ, may the Aluahim of all bless you and strengthen you to do righteousness, and His will before Him, and may He choose you and your offspring that you may become a peopl e for His inheritance according to His will always.

My son, Ya'aqoḇ, draw near and kiss me." And he drew near and kissed him, and he said, "Baruk be my son Ya'aqoḇ and all the sons of Aluahim Most High, to all the ages. May Aluahim give to you an offspring of righteousness; and some of your sons may He sanctify throughout the whole earth. May nations serve you, and all the nations bow themselves before your offspring.
Be strong in the presence of men, and exercise authority over all the offspring of Sheth. Then your ways and the ways of your sons will be justified, so that they shall become a holy nation.
May the Most High Aluahim give you all the berakoth with which He has baruk me and He baruk Noaḥ and Adawm. May they rest on the sacred head of your offspring from gene ration to generation forever.
May He cleanse you from all unrighteousness and impurity so that you may be forgiven all the transgressions, which you have committed ignorantly. May He strengthen you, and bless you.

May you inherit the whole earth, and may He renew His berith with you so that you may be to Him a nation for His inheritance for all the ages, and so that He may be to you and to your offspring a Aluahim in truth and righteousness throughout all the days of the earth.

My son Ya'aqoḇ, remember my words. Observe the comma ndments of Aḇraham, your father, separate yourself from the nations (gentiles), and do not eat with them. Do not emulate their works, and do not associate with them because their works are unclean, and all their ways are a pollution and an abomination and uncleanness.
They offer their sacrifices to the dead and they worship evil ruchot, and they eat over the graves, and all their works are empty and nothingness .

They have no heart to understand and their eyes do not see what their works are, and how they go astray by saying to a piece of wood, "You are my Aluahim," and to a stone, "You are my Yahuah and you are my deliverer," because the stone and wood have no heart.
And as for you, my son Ya'aqoḇ, may the Most High Aluahim help you and the Aluahim of shamayim bless you and remove you from their uncleanness and from all their error.
Ya'aqoḇ, be warned. Do not take a wife from any offspring of the daughters of Kena'an, for all his offspring are to be rooted out of the earth.

Because of the transgression of Ḥam, Kena'an erred, and all his offspring shall be destroyed from the earth including any remnant of it, and none springing from him shall exist except on the day of mishpat.

And as for all the worshippers of idols and the profane, there shall be no hope for them in the land of the living, and no one on earth will remember them, for they shall descend into the abode of the dead, and they shall go into the place of condemnation. As the children of Sodom were taken away from the earth, so will all those who worship idols be taken away.
Fear not, my son Ya'aqoḇ. Be not dismayed, son of Aḇraham. May the Most High Aluahim preserve you from destruction, and may He deliver you from all the paths of error.

This house have I built for myself that I might put my name on it in the earth. It is given to you and to your offspring forever, and it will be named the house of Aḇraham. It is given to you and your offspring forever, for you will build my house and establish my name before Aluahim forever. Your offspring and your name will stand throughout all generations of the earth."
He ceased commanding him and berakah him.
The two lay together on one bed, and Ya'aqoḇ slept in the embracing arms of Aḇraham, his father's father, and he kissed him seven times, and his affection and his heart rejoiced over him.

He baruk him with all his heart and said, "The Most High Aluahim, the Aluahim of all, and Creator of all, who brought me out from Ur of the Chaldees that He might give me this land to inherit forever, that I might establish a holy offspring.
Baruk be the Most High forever."
And he baruk Ya'aqoḇ and said, "May Your grace and Your mercy be lift up on my son, over whom I rejoice with all my heart and my affection and on his offspring always.

Do not forsake him, nor diminish him from now to the days of eternity, and may Your eyes be opened on him and on his offspring, that You may preserve him, and bless him, and may sanc tify him as a nation for Your inheritance. Bless him with all Your berakoth from now to all the days of eternity, and renew Your berith and Your grace with him and with his offspring according to all Your good pleasure to all the generations of the earth." [
He placed Ya'aqoḇ's two fingers on his eyes, and he baruk the Aluahim of Aluahims, and he covered his face and stretched out his feet and slept the sleep of eternity, and was gathered to his fathers.
In spite of all this, Ya'aqoḇ was lying in his embracing arms, and knew not that Aḇraham, his father's father, was dead.
Ya'aqoḇ awoke from his sleep, and realized Aḇraham was cold as ice, and he said, "Father, father," but there was no answer, and he knew that he was dead.

He arose from his embracing arms and ran and told Riḇqah, his mother, and Riḇqah went to Yitsḥaq in the night, and told him and they went together, and Ya'aqoḇ with them, and a lamp was in his hand, and when they had gone in they found Aḇraham lying dead.
Yitsḥaq fell on the face of his father and wept and kissed him.
Yishma'al, his son, heard the voices in the house of Aḇraham, and he arose, and went to Aḇraham his father, and wept over Aḇraham his father, he and all the house of Aḇraham, and they wept greatly.

His sons, Yitsḥaq and Yishma'al, buried him in the double cave, near Sarah his wife, and all the men of his house, and Yitsḥaq and Yishma'al, and all their sons, and all the sons of Keturah in their places wept for him forty days and then the days of weeping for Aḇraham were ended.
He lived three jubilees and four weeks of years, one hundred and seventy­ five years, and completed the days of his life, being old and full of days.

For the days of the lives of their forefathers were nineteen jubilees; and after the Flood they began to grow less than nineteen jubilees, and to decrease in jubilees, and to grow old quickly, and to be full of their days because of the many types of hardships and the wickedness of their ways, with the exception of Aḇraham.

For Aḇraham was perfect in all his deeds with Yahuah, and well­ pleasing in righteousness all the days of his life. Yet, he did not compl ete four jubilees in his life, when he had grown old because of the wickedness in the world, and was full of his days.

All the generations which shall arise from this time until the day of the great mishpat shall grow old quickly, before they compl ete two jubilees, and their knowledg e shall forsake them because of their old age and all their knowledge shall vanish away.

In those days, if a man lives a jubilee and a-half of years, they shall say regarding him, "He has lived long," and the greater part of his days are pain and sorrow and hardship, and there is no peace. For calamity follows on calamity, and wound on wound, and hardship on hardship, and evil deeds on evil deeds, and illness on illness, and all mishpatim of destruction such as these, piled one on another, illness and overthrow, and snow and frost and ice, and fever, and chills, and mental and physical incapac ity, and famine, and death, and sword, and captivity, and all kinds of calamities and pains.
All of these shall come on an evil generation, which transgresses on the earth. Their works are uncleanness and fornication, and pollution and abominations.

Then they shall say, "The days of the forefathers were many, lasting a thousand years, and were good; but the days of our lives, if a man lives a long life are three score years and ten, and, if he is strong, four score years, and those evil, and there is no peace in the days of this evil gene ration."

In that generation the sons shall convict their fathers and their elders of sin and unrighteousness, and of the words of their mouths and the great wickedness which they perform, and concerning their forsaking the berith which Yahuah made between them and Him. They should observe and do all His comm andments and His ordinances and all His laws, without departing either to the right hand or the left.
For all have done evil, and every mouth speaks sinfully and all their works are unclean and an abomination, and all their ways are poll ution, uncleanness, and destruction.

The earth shall be destroyed because of all their works, and there shall be no fruit (seed) of the vine, and no oil; for their actions are altogether faithless, and they shall all perish together, beasts and cattle and birds, and all the fish of the sea, because of the children of men.

They shall quarrel with one another, the youn g with the old, and the old with the young, the poor with the rich, the lowly with the great, and the beggar with the prince, because of the law and the berith; for they have forgotten the mitsvot, and berith, and feasts, and months, and Shabbaths, and jubilees, and all mishpatim.
They shall use swords and war to turn them back to the way, but they shall not return until much blood has been shed on the earth, one by another.

Those who have escaped shall not return from their wickedness to the way of righteousness, but they shall all raise themselves to a high status through deceit and wealth, that they may each steal all that belongs of his neighbor, and they shall name the great name (of Aluahim), but not in truth and not in righteousness, and they shall defile the holy of holies with their uncleanness and the corruption of their pollution.
A great punishment shall come because of the deeds of this generation, and Yahuah will give them over to the sword and to mishpat and to slavery, and to be plundered and consumed.

And He will arouse the Gentile sinners against them, who have neither mercy nor compassion, and who shall respect no one, neither old nor young, nor any one, for they are more wicked, strong, and evil than all the children of men.
They shall use violence against Yasharal and shall violate Ya'aqoḇ, and much blood shall be shed on the earth, and there shall be none to gather the dead and none to bury them.
In those days they shall cry aloud, and call and pray that they may be saved from the hand of the sinners, the Gentiles. But none shall be excluded (none shall be saved).

The heads of the children shall be white with grey hair, and a child of three weeks shall appear old like a man of one hundred years, and their work and worth shall be destroyed by hardship and oppression.
In those days the children shall begin to study the laws, and to seek the mitsvot, and to return to the path of righteousness.
The days shall begin to grow many and increase among those children of men until their days draw close to one thousand years, and to a greater number of years than before age was recorded.
There shall be neither old man nor one who is aged, for all shall be as children and youths.
All their days shall be full and they shall live in peace and in joy, and there shall be neither Satan nor any evil destroyer because all their days shall be days of berakah and healing.

And at that time Yahuah will heal His servants, and they shall rise up and see great peace, and drive out their adversaries. The righteous shall understand and be thankful, and rejoice with joy forever and ever, and they shall see all their mishpatim and all their curses enacted on their enemies.

Their bones shall rest in the earth, and their ruchot shall have much joy, and they shall know that it is Yahuah who executes mishpat, and shows mercy to hundreds and thousands and to all that love Him.
Mosheh, write down these words. Write them and record them on the heavenly tablets for a eduth for the generations forever. [

It happened after the death of Aḇraham, that Yahuah baruk Yitsḥaq his son, who arose from Hebron and went and dwelt at the Well of the Vision in the first year of the third week of this jubilee, seven years.
In the first year of the fourth week a famine began in the land, besides the first famine, which had been in the days of Aḇraham.
Ya'aqoḇ made lentil soup, and Ĕsaw came from the field hungry. He said to Ya'aqoḇ his brother, "Give me some of this red soup."
Ya'aqoḇ said to him, "Sell to me your birthright and I will give you bread, and also some of this lentil soup." And Ĕsaw said in his heart, "If I shall die what good is my birthright to me?"
He said to Ya'aqoḇ, "I give it to you." And Ya'aqoḇ said, "Swear to me, this day," and he swore to him.

And Ya'aqoḇ gave his brother Ĕsaw bread and soup, and he ate until he was satisfied, and Ĕsaw despised his birthright. For this reason was Ĕsaw's name called Edom (red), because of the red soup which Ya'aqoḇ gave him for his birthright.
And Ya'aqoḇ became the elder, and Ĕsaw was brought down from his dignity.
The famine covered the land, and Yitsḥaq departed to go down into Mitsrayim in the second year of this week, and went to the king of the Philistines to Gerar, into the presence of Abimelech.
Yahuah appeared to him and said to him, "Do not go down into Mitsrayim. Dwell in the land that I shall tell you of, and sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and bless you.

For to you and to your offspring will I give all this land, and I will establish My oath which I swore to Aḇraham your father, and I will multiply your offspring as the stars of shamayim, and will give to your offspring all this land.

And in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be baruk, because your father obeyed My voice, and kept My ways and My mitsvot, and My laws, and My ordinances, and My berith; and now do as you are told and dwell in this land."
And he dwelt in Gelar three weeks of years. And Abimelech commanded concerning him, and concerning all that was his, saying, "Any man that shall touch him or anything that is his shall surely die."
Yitsḥaq grew strong among the Philistines, and he got many possessions, oxen and sheep and camels and donkeys and a great household.
He sowed in the land of the Philistines and brought in a hundred-fold, and Yitsḥaq became very great, and the Philistines envied him.
Now all the wells that the servants of Aḇraham had dug during the life of Aḇraham, the Philistines had stopped them after the death of Aḇraham, and filled them with dirt.
Abimelech said to Yitsḥaq, "Go from us, for you are much mightier than we." Yitsḥaq departed from that place in the first year of the seventh week, and sojourned in the valleys of Gerar.

And they dug the wells of water again which the servants of Aḇraham, his father, had dug, and which the Philistines had filled after the death of Aḇraham his father, and he called their names as Aḇraham his father had named them.

The servants of Yitsḥaq dug a well in the valley, and found fresh, flowing water, and the shepherds of Gerar bickered with the shepherds of Yitsḥaq, saying, "The water is ours." Yitsḥaq called the name of the well "Pervers ity," because they had been perverse with us.
And they dug a second well, and they fought for that also, and he called its name "En mity."

He left that place and they dug another well, and for that they did not fight, and he called the name of it "Room," and Yitsḥaq said, "Now Yahuah has made room for us, and we have increased in the land."
And he went up from that place to the Well of the Oath in the first year of the first week in the forty-fourth jubilee.

Yahuah appeared to him in the night of the new moon of the first month, and said to him, "I am the Aluahim of Aḇraham your father; fear not, for I am with you, and shall bless you and shall su rely multiply your offspring as the sand of the earth, for the sake of Aḇraham my servant."
And he built an mizbe'ach there, which Aḇraham his father had first built, and he called on the name of Yahuah, and he offered sacrifice to the Aluahim of Aḇraham his father.
They dug a well and they found fresh, flowing water.

The servants of Yitsḥaq dug another well and did not find water, and they went and told Yitsḥaq that they had not found water, and Yitsḥaq said, "I have sworn this day to the Philistines and this thing has been announced to us."
And he called the name of that place the Well of the Oath, because there he had sworn to Abimelech and Ahuzzath, his friend, and also to Phicol, who was the commander and his host.
Yitsḥaq knew that day that he had sworn to them under pressure to make peace with them.

On that day Yitsḥaq cursed the Philistines and said, "Cursed be the Philistines to the day of wrath and indignation from among all nations. May Aluahim make them a disdain and a curse and an object of anger and indignation in the hands of the Gentile sinners and in the hands of the Kittim.

Whoever escapes the sword of the enemy and the Kittim, may the righteous nation root them out in mishpat from under shamayim. They shall be the enemies and foes of my children throughout their generations on the earth.

No part of them will remain. Not even one shall be spared on the day of the wrath of mishpat. The offspring of the Philistines will experience destruction, rooting out, and expulsion from the earth and this is all that is in store for them. There shall not be a name or an offspring left on the earth for these Caphtorim (the seat of the Philistine state).

For though he rises up to shamayim, he shall be brought down, and though he makes himself strong on earth, from there shall he be dragged out, and though he hide himself among the nations, even from that place shall he be rooted out.
Though he descends into the abode of the dead, his condemnation shall be great, and he shall have no peace there.

If he goes into captivity by the hands of those that seek his life they shall kill him on the way (to his imprisonment), and neither his name nor offspring shall be left on all the earth. Into an eternal curse shall he depart."
It is written and engraved concerning him on the heavenly tablets, that on the day of ju dgment he will be rooted out of the earth. [

In the second year of this week in this jubilee, Riḇqah called Ya'aqoḇ her son, and spoke to him, saying, "My son, do not take a wife from the daughters of Kena'an as Ĕsaw, your brother, who took two wives of the daughters of Kena'an, and they have made my nephesh bitter with all their unclean acts, for all their actions are fornication and lust, and there is no righteousness in them, because their deeds are evil.
I love you greatly, my son, and my heart and my affection bless you every hour of the day and in every night.
Now, my son, listen to my voice, and do the will of your mother, and do not take a wife of the daughters of this land, but only from the house of my father, and of those related to my father.

If you will take you a wife of the house of my father, the Most High Aluahim will bless you, and your children shall be a righteous generation and a holy offspring." And then spoke Ya'aqoḇ to Riḇqah, his mother, and said to her, "Look, mother, I am nine weeks of years old, and I have neither been with nor have I touched any woman, nor have I engaged myself to any, nor I have even thought of taking me wife of the daughters of Kena'an.

For I remember, mother, the words of Aḇraham, our father, for he commanded me not to take a wife of the daughters of Kena'an, but to take me a wife from the offspring of my father's house and from my kind folks.
I have heard before that daughters have been born to Laban, your brother, and I have set my heart on them to take a wife from among them.

For this reason I have guarded myself in my spirit against sinning or being corrupted in any way throughout all the days of my life; for with regard to lust and fornication, Aḇraham, my father, gave me many commands.

Despite all that he has commanded me, these two and twenty years my brother has argued with me, and spoken frequently to me and said, "My brother, take a wife that is a sister of my two wives," but I refused to do as he has done.
I swear before you mother, that all the days of my life I will not take me a wife from the daughters of the offspring of Kena'an, and I will not act wickedly as my brother has done.
Do not be afraid mother, be assured that I shall do your will and walk in uprightness, and not corrupt my ways forever."

When she heard this, she lifted up her face to shamayim and extended the fingers of her hands, and opened her mouth and baruk the Most High Aluahim, who had created the shamayim and the earth, and she gave Him thanks and praise.

She said, "Baruk be Yahuah Aluahim, and may His holy name be baruk forever and ever. He has given me Ya'aqoḇ as a pure son and a holy offspring; for he is Yours, and Yours shall his offspring be continually, throughout all the generations forever.
Bless him,

At that hour, when the spirit of righteousness descended into her mouth, she placed both her hands on the head of Ya'aqoḇ, and said, "Baruk are You, Yahuah of righteousness and Aluahim of the ages, and may You bless him beyond all the generations of men.
My Son, may He give you the path of righteousness, and reveal righteousness to your offspring.

May He make your sons many during your life, and may they arise according to the number of the months of the year. And may their sons become many and great beyond the stars of shamayim, and may their numbers be more than the sand of the sea.
May He give them this good and pleasing land, as He said He would give it to Aḇraham and to his offspring after him always, and may they hold it as a possession forever.
My son, may I see baruk children born to you during my life, and may all your offspring be baruk and holy.

And as you have refreshed your mother's spirit during her life, the womb of her that gave birth to you blesses you now. My affection and my heart (breasts) bless you and my mouth and my tongue greatly praise you.
Increase and spread over the earth. May your offspring be perfect in the joy of shamayim and earth forever. May your offspring rejoice, and on the great day of peace may they have peace.

May your name and your offspring endure to all the ages, and may the Most High Aluahim be their Aluahim, and may the Aluahim of righteousness dwell with them, and may His sanctuary be built by you all the ages.
Baruk be he that blesses you, and all flesh that curses you falsely, may it be cursed."
And she kissed him, and said to him, "May Yahuah of the world love you as the heart of your mother and her affection rejoice in you and bless you." And she ceased from berakah. [
In the seventh year of this week Yitsḥaq called Ĕsaw, his elder son, and said to him, " I am old, my son, and my sight is dim, and I do not know the day of my death.

Now, take your hunting weapons, your quiver, and your bow, and go out to the field, and hunt and catch me venison, my son, and make me flavorful meat, like my nephesh loves, and bring it to me that I may eat, and that my nephesh may bless you before I die."
But Riḇqah heard Yitsḥaq speaking to Ĕsaw.
Ĕsaw went out early to the field to hunt and catch and bring home meat to his father.

Riḇqah called Ya'aqoḇ, her son, and said to him, "Look, I heard Yitsḥaq, your father, speak to Ĕsaw, your brother, saying, "Hunt for me, and make me flavorful meat, and bring it to me that I may eat and bless you before Yahuah before I die."

Now, my son, do as you are told and do as I command you. Go to your flock and fetch me two good kids of the goats, and I will make them good tasting meat for your father, like he loves, and you will bring it to your father that he may eat and bless you to Yahuah before he dies."
Ya'aqoḇ said to Riḇqah his mother, "Mother, I shall not withhold anything which my father would eat and which would please him, but I am afraid that he will recognize my voice and wish to touch me.

And you know that I am smooth, and Ĕsaw, my brother, is hairy, and I he will see me an evildoer because I am doing something that he has not told me to do and he will be very angry with me, and I shall bring on myself a curse, and not a berakah."
Riḇqah, his mother, said to him, "Your curse be on me, my son, just do as you are told."
Ya'aqoḇ obeyed the voice of Riḇqah, his mother, and went and brought back two good and fat goat kids, and brought them to his mother, and his mother made them tasty meat like he loved.

Riḇqah took the good and pleasing clothes of Ĕsaw, her elder son, which was with her in the house, and she clothed Ya'aqoḇ, her younger son, with them, and she put the skins of the kids on his hands and on the exposed parts of his neck.
And she gave the meat and the bread, which she had prepared, to her son Ya'aqoḇ.
Ya'aqoḇ went in to his father and said, "I am your son. I have done as you asked me. Arise and sit and eat of that which I have caught, father, that your nephesh may bless me."
Yitsḥaq said to his son, "How have you found game so quickly, my son?"
Ya'aqoḇ said, "Because Yahuah your Aluahim caused me to find."
Yitsḥaq said to him, "Come closer, that I may feel you, my son, and know if you are my son Ĕsaw or not." 53 5

Ya'aqoḇ went near to Yitsḥaq, his father, and he felt him and said, "The voice is Ya'aqoḇ's voice, but the hands are the hands of Ĕsaw," and he did not recognize him, because it was a decision from shamayim to remove his power of perception and Yitsḥaq discerned not, because his hands were hairy as his brother Ĕsaw's, so Yitsḥaq baruk him.
He said, "Are you my son Ĕsaw? " and Ya'aqoḇ said, "I am your son," and Yitsḥaq said, "Bring it to me that I may eat of that which you have caught, my son, that my nephesh may bless you."
And Ya'aqoḇ brought it to him, and he ate, and Ya'aqoḇ brought him wine and he drank.
Yitsḥaq, his father, said to him, "Come close and kiss me, my son."
He came close and kissed Yitsḥaq. And he smelled the smell of his raiment, and he baruk Ya'aqoḇ and said, "Look, the smell of my son is as the smell of a full field which Yahuah has baruk.
May Yahuah give you of the dew of shamayim and of the dew of the earth, and plenty of corn and oil. Let nations serve you and peopl es bow down to you.

Be ruler over your brothers, and let your mother's sons bow down to you; and may all the berakoth that Yahuah has baruk me and baruk Aḇraham, my father, be imparted to you and to your offspring forever. Cursed be he that curses you, and baruk be he that blesses you."
It happened as soon as Yitsḥaq had made an end of berakah his son Ya'aqoḇ, that Ya'aqoḇ had went away from Yitsḥaq his father and hid himself.

Ĕsaw, his brother, came in from his hunting. And he also made flavorful meat, and brought it to his father and Ĕsaw said to his father, "Let my father arise, and eat of my venison that your nephesh may bless me."
Yitsḥaq, his father, said to him, "Who are you?" Ĕsaw said to him, "I am your first born, your son Ĕsaw. I have done as you have commanded me."

Yitsḥaq was very greatly surprised, and said, "Who is he that has hunted and caught and brought it to me, and I have eaten of all before you carne, and have baruk him, and he shall be baruk, and all his offspring forever."
It happened when Ĕsaw heard the words of his father Yitsḥaq that he cried with a very loud and bitter cry, and said to his father, "Bl ess me also, father!"
Yitsḥaq said to him, "Your brother came with trickery, and has taken away your berakah."
He said, "Now I know why his name is Ya'aqoḇ. Behold, he has supp lanted me these two times, he took away my birth-right, and now he has taken away my berakah."

Ĕsaw said, "Have you not reserved a berakah for me, father?" and Yitsḥaq answered and said to Ĕsaw, "Look, I have made him your Yahuah, and all his brothers have I given to him for servants. I have strengthened him with plenty of corn and wine and oil. Now what shall I do for you, my son?"
Ĕsaw said to Yitsḥaq, his father, "Have you only one berakah, father? Please. Bless me, also, father."
Ĕsaw lifted up his voice and wept. And Yitsḥaq answered and said to him, "Far from the dew of the earth shall be your dwelling, and far from the dew of shamayim from above.
By your sword will you live, and you will serve your brother.

It shall happen that when you become great, and do shake his yoke from off your neck, you will sin compl etely and commit a sin worthy of death, and your offspring shall be rooted out from under shamayim."
Ĕsaw kept threatening Ya'aqoḇ because of the berakah his father baruk him with, and he said in his heart, "May the days of mourn ing for my father come now, so that I may kill my brother Ya'aqoḇ." [
Riḇqah was told Ĕsaw's words in a dream, and Riḇqah sent for Ya'aqoḇ her younger son, and said to him, "Look, Ĕsaw, your brother, will take vengeance on you and kill you.

Now, therefore, my son, do as you are told, and get up and flee to Laban, my brother, to Haran, and stay with him a few days until your brother's anger fades away, and he removes his anger from you, and forgets all that you have done. Then I will send for you to come from that place."
Ya'aqoḇ said, "I am not afraid. If he wishes to kill me, I will kill him."
But she said to him, "Let me not be bereft of both my sons on one day."

Ya'aqoḇ said to Riḇqah, his mother, "Look, you know that my father has become old, and does not see because his eyes are dull. If I leave him he will think it is wrong. If I leave him and go away from you, my father will be angry and will curse me.
I will not go. When he sends me, only then will I go."
Riḇqah said to Ya'aqoḇ, "I will go in and speak to him, and he will send you away."

Riḇqah went in and said to Yitsḥaq, "I hate my life because of the two daughters of Heth, whom Ĕsaw has taken as wives. If Ya'aqoḇ take a wife from among the daughters of the land such as these, I could not live with it, because the daughters of Kena'an are evil."

Yitsḥaq called Ya'aqoḇ and baruk him, and warned him and said to him, "Do not take you a wife of any of the daughters of Kena'an. Arise and go to Mesopotamia, to the house of Bethuel, your mother's father, and take a wife from that place of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.

And Aluahim Almighty bless you and increase and multiply you that you may become a company of nations, and give you the berakoth of my father, Aḇraham, to you and to your offspring after you, that you may inherit the land that you travel in and all the land which Aluahim gave to Aḇraham. Go in peace, my son."
Yitsḥaq sent Ya'aqoḇ away, and he went to Mesopotamia, to Laban the son of Bethuel the Syrian, the brother of Riḇqah, Ya'aqoḇ's mother.
It happened after Ya'aqoḇ had departed to Mesopotamia that the spirit of Riḇqah was grieved for her son, and she wept.
Yitsḥaq said to Riḇqah, "My sister, weep not because of Ya'aqoḇ, my son, for he goes in peace, and in peace will he return.

The Most High Aluahim will preserve him from all evil and will be with him. He will not forsake him all his days, for I know that his ways will be made to prosper in all things wherever he goes, until he return in peace to us, and we see him in peace. Fear not on his accou nt, my sister, for he is on the upright path and he is a perfect man, and he is faithful and will not perish. Weep not."
Yitsḥaq comforted Riḇqah because of her son Ya'aqoḇ, and baruk him.

Ya'aqoḇ went from the Well of the Oath to go to Haran on the first year of the second week in the forty-fourth jubilee, and he came to Luz on the mountains, that is, Bethel, on the new moon of the first month of this week, and he came to the place at dusk and turned from the way to the west of the road that is close, and that night he slept there, for the sun had set.
He took one of the stones of that place (as a pillow) and laid down under the tree, and he was jou rneying alone, and he slept.
Ya'aqoḇ dreamt that night, and saw a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to shamayim, and he saw the mal'akim of Yahuah ascended and descended on it, and behold, Yahuah stood on it.
And He spoke to Ya'aqoḇ and said, "I am Yahuah Aluahim of Aḇraham, your father, and the Aluahim of Yitsḥaq. The land you are sleeping on I will give to you and to your offspring after you.

Your offspring shall be as the dust of the earth, and you will increase to the west and to the east, to the north and the south, and in you and in your offspring shall all the families of the nations be baruk.
Behold, I will be with you, and will keep you wherever you go. I will bring you into this land again in peace. I will not leave you until I do everything that I told you."
Ya'aqoḇ awoke from his sleep and said, "Trul y this place is the house of Yahuah, and I did not know it."
He was afraid and said, "I am afraid because this place is none other than the house of Aluahim, and this is the gate of shamayim, and I did not know it."

Ya'aqoḇ got up early in the morning, and took the stone that he had placed under his head and set it up as a pillar for a sign. And he poured oil on the top of it. And he called the name of that place Bethel, but the name of the place was previously Luz.

And Ya'aqoḇ vowed a vow to Yahuah, saying, "If Yahuah will be with me, and will keep me in the way that I go, and give me bread to eat and clothes to put on, so that I come again to my father's house in peace, then Yahuah shall be my Aluahim, and this stone which I have set up as a pillar for a sign in this place shall be Yahuah's house, and of all that you gave me, I shall give the tenth to you, my Aluahim." [

He went on his jou rney, and came to the land of the east, to Laban, the brother of Riḇqah, and he was with him, and Ya'aqoḇ served Laban for Rachel his daughter one week of years. In the first year of the third week of years he said to him, "Give me my wife, for whom I have served you seven years /' and Laban said to Ya'aqoḇ, "I will give you your wife."
Laban made a feast, and took Leah his elder daughter, and gave her to Ya'aqoḇ as a wife, and gave Leah Zilpah for a handmaid; and Ya'aqoḇ did not know, for he thought that she was Rachel.
He went in to her, and saw she was Leah; and Ya'aqoḇ was angry with Laban, and said to him, "Why have you done this to me?
Did I not serve you for Rachel and not for Leah? Why have you wronged me?

Take your daughter, and I will go. You have done evil to me." For Ya'aqoḇ loved Rachel more than Leah because Leah's eyes were weak, but her form was very beautiful. Rachel had beautiful eyes and a beautiful and very voluptuous form.

Laban said to Ya'aqoḇ, "It is not done that way in our coun try, we do not to give the younger before the elder." And it is not right to do this; for thus it is ordained and written in the heavenly tablets, that no one should give his younger daughter before the elder; but the elder one is given first and after her the younger. The man who does so will have guilt placed against him in shamayim, and none is righteous that does this thing, for this deed is evil before Yahuah.
Command the children of Yasharal that they not do this thing . Let them neither take nor give the younger before they have given the elder, for it is very wicked."

And Laban said to Ya'aqoḇ, "Let the seven days of the feast pass by, and I shall give you Rachet that you may serve me another seven years, that you may pasture my sheep as you did in the former week (of years)."

On the day when the seven days of the feast of Leah had passed, Laban gave Rachel to Ya'aqoḇ, that he might serve him another seven years, and he gave Rachel, Bilhah, the sister of Zilpah, as a handmaid .
He served yet other seven years for Rachel, for Leah had been given to him for nothing, since it was Rachel he wanted.

And Yahuah opened the womb of Leah, and she conceived and gave birth to a son for Ya'aqoḇ, and he called his name Re'uḇen, on the fourteenth day of the ninth month, in the first year of the third week.
But the womb of Rachel was closed, for Yahuah saw that Leah was hated and Rachel loved.

Again Ya'aqoḇ went in to Leah, and she conceived, and gave birth to a second son for Ya'aqoḇ, and he called his name Shim'on, on the twent y-first of the tenth month, and in the third year of this week.
Again Ya'aqoḇ went in to Leah, and she conceived, and gave birth to a third son, and he called his name Lewi, in the new moon of the first month in the sixth year of this week.
Again Ya'aqoḇ went in to her, and she conceived, and gave birth to a fourth son, and he called his name Yahuḏah, on the fifteenth of the third month, in the first year of the fourth week.

Because of all this Rachel envied Leah, for she did not bear a child, and she said to Ya'aqoḇ, "Give me children;" and Ya'aqoḇ said, "Have I withheld from you the fruits of your womb? Have I left you?"

And when Rachel saw that Leah had given birth to four sons for Ya'aqoḇ: Re'uḇen and Shim'on and Lewi and Yahuḏah, she said to him, "Go in to Bilhah my handmaid, and she will conceive, and bear a son for me."
