She gave him Bilhah, her handmaid, to wife. And he went in to her, and she conceived, and gave birth to a son, and he called his name Dan, on the ninth of the sixth month, in the sixth year of the third week.

Ya'aqoḇ went in again to Bilhah a second time, and she conceived, and gave birth to another son for Ya'aqoḇ, and Rachel called his name Napthali, on the fifth of the seventh month, in the second year of the fourth week.

When Leah saw that she had become sterile and could no longer have children, she envied Rachel, and she also gave her handmaid Zilpah to Ya'aqoḇ to wife, and she conceived, and gave birth to a son, and Leah called his name Gad, on the twelfth of the eighth month, in the third year of the fourth week.
He went in to her again, and she conceived and gave birth to a second son, and Leah called his name Asher, on the second of the eleventh month, in the fifth year of the fourth week.

Ya'aqoḇ went in to Leah, and she conceived, and gave birth to a son, and she called his name Issachar, on the fourth of the fifth month, in the fourth year of the fourth week, and she gave him to a nurse.

Ya'aqoḇ went in again to her, and she conceived, and gave birth to two children, a son and a daughter, and she called the name of the son Zabulon, and the name of the daughter Dinah, in the seventh day of the seventh month, in the sixth year of the fourth week.

Yahuah was gracious to Rachel, and opened her womb, and she conceived, and gave birth to a son, and she called his name Yoseph, on the new moon of the fourth month, in the sixth year in this fourth week.

In the days when Yoseph was born, Ya'aqoḇ said to Laban, "Give me my wives and sons, and let me go to my father Yitsḥaq, and let me make a household for myself; for I have completed the years in which I have served you for your two daughters, and I will go to the house of my father."
Laban said to Ya'aqoḇ, "Stay with me and I will pay you wages, and pasture my flock for me again, and take your wages."
They agreed with one another that he should give him as his wages those of the lambs and kids which were born spotted black and white, these were to be his wages.

All the sheep brought out spotted and speckled and black, variously marked, and they brought out again lambs like themselves, and all that were spotted were Ya'aqoḇ's and those which were not spotted were Laban's.
Ya'aqoḇ's possessions multiplied greatly, and he possessed oxen and sheep and donkeys and camels, and men-servants and maid-servants.
Laban and his sons envied Ya'aqoḇ, and Laban took back his sheep from him, and he envied him and watched him for an opportunity to do evil. [
It happened when Rachel had given birth to Yoseph, that Laban went to shear his sheep; for they were distant from him, a three-day journey.
Ya'aqoḇ saw that Laban was going to shear his sheep, and Ya'aqoḇ called Leah and Rachel, and spoke sweetly to them in order to convince them to come with him to the land of Kena'an.

For he told them how he had seen everything in a dream. All that Aluahim had spoken to him that he should return to his father's house, and they said, "To every place where you go we will go with you."

Ya'aqoḇ baruk the Aluahim of Yitsḥaq his father, and the Aluahim of Aḇraham his father's father, and he arose and placed his wives and his children on donkeys, and took all his possessions and crossed the river, and came to the land of Gilead, and Ya'aqoḇ hid his intention from Laban and did not tell him.
In the seventh year of the fourth week Ya'aqoḇ turned his face toward Gilead in the first month, on the twenty-first of it.
Laban pursued and overtook Ya'aqoḇ in the mountain of Gilead in the third month, on the thirteenth of it. And Yahuah did not permit him to injure Ya'aqoḇ for he appeared to him in a dream by night.

Laban spoke to Ya'aqoḇ. On the fifteenth of those days Ya'aqoḇ made a feast for Laban, and for all who came with him, and Ya'aqoḇ swore to Laban that day, and Laban also swore to Ya'aqoḇ, that neither should cross the mountain of Gilead to do evil to the other.
He made a heap (of stones) for a witness there; wherefore the name of that place is called, "The Heap of Witness," after this heap.
But before they used to call the land of Gilead the land of the Rephaim. The Rephaim were born giants whose height was ten, nine, eight, down to seven cubits.
Their dwelling place was from the land of the children of Ammon to Mount Hermon, and the seats of their kingdom were Karnaim and Ashtaroth, and Edrei, and Misur, and Beon.

Yahuah destroyed them because of the evil of their deeds, for they were malevolent, and the Amorites were wicked and sinful. There is no people today which has committed the full range of their sins, and their life on the earth was shortened.
Ya'aqoḇ sent Laban away, and he departed into Mesopotamia, the land of the East, and Ya'aqoḇ returned to the land of Gilead.

He passed over the Jabbok in the ninth month, on the eleventh of it. On that day Ĕsaw, his brother, came to him, and he was reconciled to him, and departed from him to the land of Seir, but Ya'aqoḇ dwelt in tents.

In the first year of the fifth week in this jubilee he crossed the Yarden, and dwelt beyond the Yarden. He pastured his sheep from the sea of the heap to Bethshan, and to Dothan and to the forest of Akrabbim.
He sent his father Yitsḥaq all of his possessions such as clothing, and food, and meat, and drink, and milk, and butter, and cheese, and some dates of the valley.

Four times a year, he sent gifts to his mother Riḇqah who was living at the tower of Aḇraham. He sent the gifts between the times of the months between plowing and reaping, and between autumn and the rain season, and between winter and spring.
For Yitsḥaq had returned from the Well of the Oath and gone up to the tower of his father Aḇraham, and he dwelt there apart from his son Ĕsaw.

For in the days when Ya'aqoḇ went to Mesopotamia, Ĕsaw took to himself a wife Mahalath, the daughter of Yishma'al, and he gathered together all the flocks of his father and his wives, and went up and dwelt on Mount Seir, and left Yitsḥaq his father at the Well of the Oath alone.

And Yitsḥaq went up from the Well of the Oath and dwelt in the tower of Aḇraham his father on the mountains of Hebron, and that is where Ya'aqoḇ sent all that he did send to his father and his mother from time to time, all they needed, and they baruk Ya'aqoḇ with all their heart and with all their nephesh. [

In the first year of the sixth week he went up to Salem, to the east of Shechem, in the fourth month, and he went in peace. Shechem, the son of Hamor, the Hivite, the prince of the land carried off Dinah, the daughter of Ya'aqoḇ, into the house, and he had sex with her and defiled her. She was a little girl, a child of twelve years.
He begged his father and her brothers that she might be given to him as a wife.

Ya'aqoḇ and his sons were very angry because of the men of Shechem, for they had defiled Dinah, their sister. They spoke to them while planning evil acts and they dealt deceitfully with them and tricked them.

Shim'on and Lewi came unexpectedly to Shechem and executed mishpat on all the men of Shechem, and killed all the men whom they found in it. They did not leave a single one remaining in it. They killed all in hand to hand battle because they had dishonored their sister Dinah.

Let it not again be done from now on that a daughter of Yasharal be defiled. Mishpat is ordained in shamayim against them that they should destroy all the men of the Shechemites with the sword because they had committed shame in Yasharal.

Yahuah delivered them into the hands of the sons of Ya'aqoḇ that they might exterminate them with the sword and execute mishpat on them. That it might not again be done in Yasharal that a virgin of Yasharal should be defiled.

If there is any man in Yasharal who wishes to give his daughter or his sister to any man who is of the offspring of the Gentiles he shall surely die. They shall stone him, for he has committed shame in Yasharal. They shall burn the woman with fire, because she has dishonored the name of the house of her father, and she shall be rooted out of Yasharal.

Do not let an adulteress and let no uncleanness be found in Yasharal throughout all the days of the generations of the earth. For Yasharal is holy to Yahuah, and every man who has defiled it shall surely die. They shall stone him.

For it has been ordained and written in the heavenly tablets regarding all the offspring of Yasharal. He who defiles it shall surely die. He shall be killed by stoning. There is no limit of days for this law. There is no remission, and no atonement.

The man who has defiled his daughter shall be rooted out from every corner of all Yasharal, because he has given of his offspring to Moloch (a pagan Aluahim, the worship of which involved burning the child alive), and committed impurity and defiled his child.

Mosheh, command the children of Yasharal and exhort them not to give their daughters to the Gentiles, and not to take for their sons any of the daughters of the Gentiles, for this is abominable before Yahuah.

It is because of this that I have written all the deeds of the Shechemites, which they committed against Dinah, and placed them in the words of the Law for you. I have also written how the sons of Ya'aqoḇ spoke, saying, "We will not give our daughter to a man who is uncircumcised, for that is a reproach to us."
It is a reproach to Yasharal that anyone take the daughters of the Gentiles, for this is unclean and abominable to Yasharal.
Yasharal will not be free from this uncleanness if it has a wife of the daughters of the Gentiles, or has given any of its daughters to a man who is of any of the Gentiles.

There will be plague upon plague, and curse upon curse, and every mishpat and plague and curse will come if he does this thing, or if they ignore those who commit uncleanness, or defile the sanctuary of Yahuah, or those who profane His holy name. If any of these happen the whole nation together will be judged for all the uncleanness and profanation of this man.

There will be no judging people by their position and no receiving fruits, or offerings, or burnt-offerings, or fat, or the fragrance of sweet odor from his hands. It will be unacceptable and so warn every man and woman in Yasharal who defiles the sanctuary.

For this reason I have commanded you, saying, "Give this eduth to Yasharal, see how the Shechemites and their sons fared? See how they were delivered into the hands of two sons of Ya'aqoḇ, and they killed them under torture? It was counted to them for righteousness, and it is written down to them for righteousness.

The offspring of Lewi were chosen for the kehunnah, and to be Levites, that they might minister before Yahuah, as we do, continually. Lewi and his sons will be baruk forever, for he was zealous to execute righteousness and mishpat and vengeance on all those who arose against Yasharal.
So they wrote a eduth in his favor of berakah and righteousness on the heavenly tablets in the presence of the Aluahim of all.

We remember the righteousness that the man fulfilled during his life, throughout the years, until a thousand generations they will record it. It will come to him and to his descendants after him, and he has been recorded on the heavenly tablets as a friend and a righteous man.

All this account I have written for you, and have commanded you to tell the children of Yasharal, so that they will not commit sin nor transgress the laws nor break the berith which has been ordained for them. They should fulfill it and be recorded as friends (of Aluahim).

But if they transgress and work uncleanness in any way, they will be recorded on the heavenly tablets as adversaries (of Aluahim), and they will be blotted out of the book of life. Instead, they will be recorded in the book of those who will be destroyed and with those who will be rooted out of the earth.

On the day when the sons of Ya'aqoḇ killed Shechem it was written in the record in their favor in shamayim that they had executed righteousness and uprightness and vengeance on the sinners, and it was written for a berakah.

They brought Dinah, their sister, out of the house of Shechem. They took everything that was in Shechem captive. They took their sheep and their oxen and their donkeys, and all their wealth, and all their flocks, and brought them all to Ya'aqoḇ their father.
He reproached them because they had put the city to the sword for he feared those who dwelt in the land, the Canaanites and the Perizzites.
The dread of Yahuah was on all the cities that are near Shechem. They did not fight or chase after the sons of Ya'aqoḇ, for terror had fallen on them. [

On the new moon of the month, Ya'aqoḇ spoke to all the people of his house, saying, "Purify yourselves and change your clothes, and let us get up and go to Bethel where I vowed a vow to Him on the day when I fled from Ĕsaw my brother. Let us do this because Aluahim has been with me and brought me into this land in peace. You must put away the strange Aluahims that you raise among you."

They gave up the strange Aluahims and that which was in their ears and which was on their necks and the idols which Rachel stole from Laban her father she gave wholly to Ya'aqoḇ. And he burnt and broke them to pieces and destroyed them, and hid them under an oak, which is in the land of Shechem.

He went up on the new moon of the seventh month to Bethel. And he built an mizbe'ach at the place where he had slept, and he set up a pillar there, and he sent word to his father, Yitsḥaq, and his mother, Riḇqah. He asked to come to Yitsḥaq. There, Ya'aqoḇ wished to offer his sacrifice.
Yitsḥaq said, "Let my son, Ya'aqoḇ, come, and let me see him before I die."
Ya'aqoḇ went to his father, Yitsḥaq, and his mother, Riḇqah, to the house of his father Aḇraham, and he took two of his sons with him, Lewi and Yahuḏah.
Riḇqah came out from the tower to the front of it to kiss Ya'aqoḇ and embrace him, for her spirit had revived when she heard, "Look Ya'aqoḇ your son has come," and she kissed him.

She saw his two sons and she recognized them. She said to him, "Are these your sons, my son?" and she embraced them and kissed them, and baruk them, saying, "In you shall the offspring of Aḇraham become illustrious, and you shall prove a berakah on the earth."

Ya'aqoḇ went in to Yitsḥaq his father, to the room where he lay, and his two sons were with him. He took his father's hand, stooped down, he kissed him. Yitsḥaq held on to the neck of Ya'aqoḇ his son, and wept on his neck.
The darkness left the eyes of Yitsḥaq, and he saw the two sons of Ya'aqoḇ, Lewi, and Yahuḏah. And he said, "Are these your sons, my son? Because they look like you."
He said to Yitsḥaq, "They were truly my sons, and you have clearly seen that they are truly my sons./I
They came near to him, and he turned and kissed them and embraced them both together.
The spirit of neḇuah came down into his mouth, and he took Lewi by his right hand and Yahuḏah by his left.
He turned to Lewi first, and began to bless him first, and said to him, "May the Aluahim of all, the very Yahuah of all the ages, bless you and your children throughout all the ages.

May Yahuah give to you and your offspring greatness and great glory from among all flesh. May Yahuah cause you and your offspring to draw near to Him to serve in His sanctuary like the mal'akim of the presence (of Yahuah) and as the holy ones. The offspring of your sons shall be for the glory and greatness and holiness of Aluahim. May He make them great throughout all the ages. They shall be judges and princes, and chiefs of all the offspring of the sons of Ya'aqoḇ. They shall speak the word of Yahuah in righteousness, and they shall judge all His mishpatim in righteousness.
They shall declare My ways to Ya'aqoḇ and My paths to Yasharal. The berakah of Yahuah shall be given in their mouths to bless all the offspring of the beloved.

Your mother has called your name Lewi, and rightly has she called your name. You will be joined to Yahuah and be the companion of all the sons of Ya'aqoḇ. Let His table be your table, and let your sons eat from it. May your table be full throughout all generations, and let your food not fail in all the ages.
Let all who hate you fall down before you, and let all your adversaries be rooted out and perish. Baruk be he that blesses you, and cursed be every nation that curses you./I

To Yahuḏah he said, "May Yahuah give you strength and power to put all that hate you under your feet. You and one of your sons will be a prince over the sons of Ya'aqoḇ. May your name and the name of your sons go out across every land and region.
Then shall the Gentiles fear you, and all the nations and people shall shake (with fear of you). In you will be the help of Ya'aqoḇ, and in you will be found the yasha of Yasharal.

When you sit on the throne, which honors of your righteousness, there shall be great peace for all the offspring of the sons of the beloved. Baruk be he that blesses you, and cursed be all that hate you, afflict you, or curse you. They shall be rooted out and destroyed from the earth./I
He turned, kissed him again, and embraced him, and rejoiced greatly because he had seen the sons of his son, Ya'aqoḇ, clearly and truly.
He stepped out from between his feet and fell down. He bowed down to him, and baruk them. He rested there with Yitsḥaq, his father, that night, and they ate and drank with joy.
He made the two sons of Ya'aqoḇ sleep, the one on his right hand and the other on his left. It was counted to him for righteousness.
Ya'aqoḇ told his father everything during the night about how Yahuah had shown him great mercy, and how he had caused him to prosper in all his ways, and how he protected him from all evil.
Yitsḥaq baruk the Aluahim of his father Aḇraham, who had not withdrawn his mercy and his righteousness from the sons of his servant Yitsḥaq.

In the morning, Ya'aqoḇ told his father, Yitsḥaq, the vow, which he had vowed to Yahuah. He told him of the vision which he had seen, and that he had built an mizbe'ach. He told him that everything was ready for the sacrifice to be made before Yahuah as he had vowed. He had come to set him on a donkey.

Yitsḥaq said to Ya'aqoḇ his son, "I am not able to go with you, for I am old and not able to endure the way. Go in peace, my son. I am one hundred and sixty-five years this day. I am no longer able to journey. Set your mother on a donkey and let her go with you.
I know that you have come on my account, my son. May this day be baruk on which you have seen me alive, and I also have seen you, my son.

May you prosper and fulfill the vow that you have vowed. Do not put off your vow, for you will be called to account for the vow. Now hurry to perform it, and may He who has made all things be pleased. It is to Him you have vowed the vow."
He said to Riḇqah, "Go with Ya'aqoḇ your son," and Riḇqah went with Ya'aqoḇ her son, and Deborah with her, and they came to Bethel.
Ya'aqoḇ remembered the prayer with which his father had baruk him and his two sons, Lewi and Yahuḏah. He rejoiced and baruk the Aluahim of his fathers, Aḇraham and Yitsḥaq.

He said, "Now I know that my sons and I have an eternal hope in the Aluahim of all." Thus is it ordained concerning the two. They recorded it as an eternal eduth to them on the heavenly tablets how Yitsḥaq baruk his sons. [
That night he stayed at Bethel, and Lewi dreamed that they had ordained and made his sons and him the kohanim of the Most High Aluahim forever. Then he awoke from his sleep and baruk Yahuah.

Ya'aqoḇ rose early in the morning, on the fourteenth of this month, and he gave a tithe for all that came with him, both of men and cattle, both of gold and every vessel and garment. Yes, he gave tithes of all.

In those days Rachel became pregnant with her son Binyamin. Ya'aqoḇ counted his sons starting from him and going to the oldest and Lewi fell to the portion of Yahuah. (Lewi was the third son - three is the number of spiritual completeness.) His father clothed him in the garments of the kehunnah and filled his hands.

On the fifteenth of this month, he brought fourteen oxen from among the cattle, and twenty-eight rams, and forty-nine sheep, and seven lambs, and twenty-one kids of the goats to the mizbe'ach as a burnt-offering on the mizbe'ach of sacrifice. The offering was well pleasing and a sweet odor before Aluahim. S This was his offering, done in acknowledgement of the vow in which he had promised that he would give a tenth, with their fruit-offerings and their drink- offerings.

When the fire had consumed it, he burnt incense over the fire, and for a thank-offering he sacrificed two oxen and four rams and four sheep, four male goats, and two sheep of a year old, and two kids of the goats. This he did daily for seven days.
He, his men, and all his sons were eating this with joy during seven days and berakah and thanking Yahuah, who had delivered him out of all his tribulation and had given him His promise.

He tithed all the clean animals, and made a burnt sacrifice, but he did not give the unclean animals to Lewi his son. He gave him (responsibility for) all the nepheshoth of the men. Lewi acted in the kohenic office at Bethel in the presence of Ya'aqoḇ his father, in preference to his ten brothers. He was a kohen there, and Ya'aqoḇ gave his vow, and he gave a tithe to Yahuah again and sanctified it, and it became holy to Him.

For this reason it is ordained on the heavenly tablets as a law for the offering of the tithe should be eaten in the presence of Yahuah every year, in the place where it is chosen that His name should live and reside. This law has no limit of days forever.

This law is written so that it may be fulfilled every year. The second tithe should be eaten in the presence of Yahuah, in the place where it has been chosen, and nothing shall be left over from it from this year to the following year. n In its year shall the seed be eaten until the days of the gathering of the seed of the year. The wine shall be consumed until the days of the wine, and the oil until the days of its season.
All that is left of it and all that becomes old will be regarded as spoiled, let it be burnt with fire, for it is unclean.
Let them eat it together in the sanctuary, and let them not permit it to become old.

All the tithes of the oxen and sheep shall be holy to Yahuah, and shall belong to His kohanim. They will eat before Him from year to year, for thus is it ordained and written on the heavenly tablets regarding the tithe.

On the following night, on the twenty-second day of this month, Ya'aqoḇ resolved to build that place and to surround the court with a wall, and to sanctify it and make it holy forever, for himself and his children after him.
Yahuah appeared to him by night and baruk him and said to him, "Your name shall not be called Ya'aqoḇ, but they will call your name Yasharal."

And He said to him again, "I am Yahuah who created the shamayim and the earth, and I will increase you and multiply you greatly, and kings shall come forth from you, and they shall be judges everywhere the foot of the sons of men have walked.
I will give to your offspring all the earth that is under shamayim. They shall judge all the nations, as they desire. After that they shall possess the entire earth and inherit it forever."
And He finished speaking with him, and He went up from him.
Ya'aqoḇ watched until He had ascended into shamayim.

In a vision at night he saw an mal'ak descend from shamayim with seven tablets in his hands, and he gave them to Ya'aqoḇ, and he read them and knew all that was written on it that would happen to him and his sons throughout all the ages.

He showed him all that was written on the tablets, and said to him, "Do not build on this place, and do not make it an eternal sanctuary, and do not live here. This is not the place. Go to the house of Aḇraham your father and live with Yitsḥaq, your father, until the day he dies.
For in Mitsrayim you will die in peace, and in this land you will be buried with honor in the sepulcher of your fathers, with Aḇraham and Yitsḥaq.
Do not fear. As you have seen and read it shall all be. Write down everything that you have seen and read."
Ya'aqoḇ said, "Yahuah, how can I remember all that I have read and seen?" He said to him, "I will bring all things to your remembrance."
He ascended from Ya'aqoḇ, and Ya'aqoḇ awoke from his sleep. He remembered everything that he had read and seen, and he wrote down all the words.

He celebrated there yet another day, and he sacrificed on that day as he had sacrificed on all the former days. He called its name "Addition," because this day was added, and the former days he called "The Feast."
It was made known and revealed to him and it is written on the heavenly tablets that he should celebrate the day, and add it to the seven days of the feast.
Its name was called " Addition," because that it was recorded among the days of the feast days, according to the number of the days of the year.

In the night, on the twenty-third of this month, Deborah, Riḇqah's nurse died, and they buried her beneath the city under the oak of the river. He called the name of this place, "The river of Deborah," and he called the oak, "The oak of the mourning of Deborah."
Riḇqah departed and returned to her house, to his father Yitsḥaq. Ya'aqoḇ sent rams and sheep and male goats by her so that she should prepare a meal for his father such as he desired.
He followed his mother until he came to the land of Kabratan, and he lived there.

Rachel gave birth to a son in the night, and called his name "son of my sorrow", for she broke down while giving birth to him, but his father called his name Binyamin. This happened on the eleventh day of the eighth month in the first of the sixth week of this jubilee.
Rachel died there and she was buried in the land of Ephrath, the same is Bethlehem, and Ya'aqoḇ built a pillar on the grave of Rachel, on the road above her grave. [
Ya'aqoḇ went and lived to the south of Magdaladra'ef. He and Leah, his wife, went to his father, Yitsḥaq, on the new moon of the tenth month.
Re'uḇen saw Bilhah, Rachel's maid, the concubine of his father, bathing in water in a secret place, and he loved her.
He hid himself at night, and he entered the house of Bilhah at night. He found her sleeping alone on a bed in her house.
He had sex with her. She awoke and saw that is was Re'uḇen lying with her in the bed. She uncovered the border of her covering and grabbed him and cried out when she discovered that it was Re'uḇen.
She was ashamed because of him and released her hand from him, and he fled.
Because of this, she mourned greatly and did not tell it to anyone.

When Ya'aqoḇ returned and sought her, she said to him, "I am not clean for you. I have been defiled in regard to you. Re'uḇen has defiled me, and has had sex with me in the night. I was asleep and did not realize he was there until he uncovered my skirt and had sex with me."
Ya'aqoḇ was very angry with Re'uḇen because he had sex with Bilhah, because he had uncovered his father's skirt.
Ya'aqoḇ did not approach her again because Re'uḇen had defiled her. And as for any man who uncovers his father's skirt his deed is greatly wicked, for he is disgusting to Yahuah.

For this reason it is written and ordained on the heavenly tablets that a man should not lie with his father's wife, and should not uncover his father's skirt. This is unclean and they shall surely die together, the man who lies with his father's wife and the woman also, for they have committed uncleanness on the earth.
There shall be nothing unclean before our Aluahim in the nation that He has chosen for Himself as a possession.
Again, it is written a second time, "Cursed be he who lies with the wife of his father, for he has uncovered his father's shame." All the holy ones of Yahuah said, "So be it. So be it."

"Mosheh, command the children of Yasharal so that they observe this word. It entails a punishment of death. It is unclean, and there is no atonement forever for the man who has committed this. He is to be put to death. Kill him by stoning. Root him out from among the people of our Aluahim.
No man who does so in Yasharal will be permitted to remain alive a single day on the earth. He is abominable and unclean.
Do not let them say, "Re'uḇen was granted life and forgiveness after he had sex with his father's concubine, although she had a husband, and her husband, Ya'aqoḇ, his father, was still alive."

Until that time the ordinance and mishpat and law had not been revealed in its completeness for all. In your days it has been revealed as a law of seasons and of days. It is an everlasting law for all generations forever. For this law has no limit of days, and no atonement for it.
They must both be rooted out of the entire nation. On the day they committed it they shall be killed.

Mosheh, write it down for Yasharal that they may observe it, and do according to these words, and not commit a sin punishable by death. Yahuah our Aluahim is judge, who does not respect persons (position) and accepts no gifts.

Tell them these words of the berith, that they may hear and observe, and be on their guard with respect to them, and not be destroyed and rooted out of the land; for an uncleanness, and an abomination, and a contamination, and a pollution are all they who commit it on the earth before our Aluahim.

There is no greater sin on earth than fornication that they commit. Yasharal is a holy nation to Yahuah its Aluahim, and a nation of inheritance. It is a kohenic and royal nation and for His own possession. There shall appear no such uncleanness among the holy nation.
In the third year of this sixth week, Ya'aqoḇ and all his sons went and lived in the house of Aḇraham, near Yitsḥaq his father and Riḇqah his mother.

These were the names of the sons of Ya'aqoḇ, the first-born Re'uḇen, Shim'on, Lewi, Yahuḏah, Issachar, Zebulon, which are the sons of Leah. The sons of Rachel are Yoseph and Binyamin. The sons of Bilhah are Dan and Naphtali; and the sons of Zilpah, Gad and Asher. Dinah is the daughter of Leah, the only daughter of Ya'aqoḇ.

They came and bowed themselves to Yitsḥaq and Riḇqah. When they saw them they baruk Ya'aqoḇ and all his sons, and Yitsḥaq rejoiced greatly, for he saw the sons of Ya'aqoḇ, his younger son and he baruk them. [
In the sixth year of this week of the forty-fourth jubilee Ya'aqoḇ sent his sons and his servants to pasture their sheep in the pastures of Shechem.
The seven kings of the Amorites assembled themselves together (to fight) against them and kill them. They hid themselves under the trees, to take their cattle as booty.

Ya'aqoḇ, Lewi, Yahuḏah and Yoseph were in the house with Yitsḥaq their father, for his spirit was sorrowful, and they could not leave him. Binyamin was the youngest, and for this reason he remained with his father.

The king of Taphu, the king of Aresa, the king of Seragan, the king of Selo, the king of Ga' as, the king of Bethoron, the king of Ma' anisakir, and all those who dwell in these mountains and who dwell in the woods in the land of Kena'an came. S They announced to Ya'aqoḇ saying, "Look, the kings of the Amorites have surrounded your sons, and plundered their herds."
And he left his house, he and his three sons and all the servants of his father, and his own servants, and he went against them with six thousand men, who carried swords.

He killed them in the pastures of Shechem, and pursued those who fled, and he killed them with the edge of the sword, and he killed Aresa and Taphu and Saregan and Selo and Amani sakir and Gaga' as, and he recovered his herds.
He conquered them, and imposed tribute on them that they should pay him five fruit products of their land. He built (the cities of) Robel and Tamnatares.
He returned in peace, and made peace with them, and they became his servants until the day that he and his sons went down into Mitsrayim.
In the seventh year of this week he sent Yoseph from his house to the land of Shechem to learn about the welfare of his brothers. He found them in the land of Dothan.

They dealt treacherously with him, and formed a plot against him to kill him, but they changed their minds and sold him to Ishmaelite merchants. They brought him down into Mitsrayim, and they sold him to Potiphar, the eunuch of Pharaoh, the chief of the cooks and kohen of the city of Elew."
The sons of Ya'aqoḇ slaughtered a kid, and dipped Yoseph's coat in the blood and sent it to Ya'aqoḇ their father on the tenth of the seventh month.

They brought it to him in the evening and he mourned all that night. He became feverish with mourning for Yoseph's death, and he said, "An evil beast has devoured Yoseph". All the members of his house mourned and grieved with him that day.
His sons and his daughter got up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted for his son.
On that day Bilhah heard that Yoseph had perished, and she died mourning him. She was living in Qafratef, and Dinah, his daughter, died after Yoseph had perished.
There were now three reasons for Yasharal to mourn in one month. They buried Bilhah next to the tomb of Rachel, and Dinah, his daughter. They were (all) buried there.
He mourned for Yoseph one year, and did not cease, for he said, "Let me go down to my grave mourning for my son."

For this reason it is ordained for the children of Yasharal that they should remember and mourn on the tenth of the seventh month. On that day the news came which made Ya'aqoḇ weep for Yoseph. On this day they should make atonement for their sins for themselves with a young goat on the tenth of the seventh month, once a year, for they had grieved the sorrow of their father regarding Yoseph his son.
This day, once a year, has been ordained that they should grieve on it for their sins, and for all their transgressions and for all their errors, so that they might cleanse themselves.

After Yoseph perished, the sons of Ya'aqoḇ took to themselves wives. The name of Re'uḇen's wife is Ada; and the name of Shim'on's wife is Adlba' a, a Canaanite. The name of Lewi's wife is Melka, of the daughters of Aram, of the offspring of the sons of Terah. The name of Yahuḏah's wife is Betasu'el, a Canaanite. The name of Issachar's wife is Hezaqa, and the name of Zabulon's wife is Ni'iman. The name of Dan's wife is Egla. The name of Naphtali's wife is Rasu'u, of Mesopotamia. The name of Gad's wife is Maka. The name of Asher's wife is Ijona. The name of Yoseph's wife is Asenath, the Mitsrite. The name of Binyamin's wife is Ijasaka.
And Shim'on repented, and took a second wife from Mesopotamia as his brothers had done. [

In the first year of the first week of the forty-fifth jubilee Riḇqah called Ya'aqoḇ, her son, and commanded him regarding his father and regarding his brother, that he should honor them all the days of his life.
Ya'aqoḇ said, "I will do everything you have commanded. I will honor them. This will be honor and greatness to me, and righteousness before Yahuah.
Mother, you know from the time I was born until this day, all my deeds and all that is in my heart. I always think good concerning all.
Why should I not do this thing which you have commanded me, that I should honor my father and my brother?
Tell me, mother, what perversity have you seen in me and I shall turn away from it, and mercy will be on me."

She said to him, "My son, in all my days I have not seen any perverseness in you, but only upright deeds. Yet, I will tell you the truth, my son, I shall die this year. I shall not survive this year in my life. I have seen the day of my death in a dream. I should not live beyond a hundred and fifty-five years. I have completed all the days that I am to live my life."

Ya'aqoḇ laughed at the words of his mother because his mother had said she should die. She was sitting across from him in possession of her strength, and she was still strong. She came and went (as she wished). She could see well, and her teeth were strong. No sickness had touched her all the days of her life.

Ya'aqoḇ said to her, "If my days of life are close to yours and my strength remains with me as your strength has, I would be baruk, mother. You will not die. You are simply joking with me regarding your death."

She went in to Yitsḥaq and said to him, " I make one request of you. Make Ĕsaw swear that he will not injure Ya'aqoḇ, nor pursue him with intent to harm him. You know Ĕsaw's thoughts have been perverse from his youth, and there is no goodness in him. He desires to kill him after you die.

You know all that he has done since the day Ya'aqoḇ, his brother, went to Haran until this day. He has forsaken us with his whole heart, and has done evil to us. He has stolen your flocks and carried off all your possessions while you watched.
When we asked him and begged him for what was our own, he did as a man (stranger) who was taking pity on us (giving a token like one giving alms to a beggar).

He is bitter against you because you baruk Ya'aqoḇ, your perfect and upright son. There is no evil but only goodness in Ya'aqoḇ. Since he came from Haran to this day he has not robbed us of anything. He always brings us everything in its season. He rejoices and blesses us with all his heart when we take his hands. He has not parted from us since he came from Haran until this day, and he remains with us continually at home honoring us."

Yitsḥaq said to her, "I also know and see the deeds of Ya'aqoḇ who is with us, how he honors us with all his heart. Before, I loved Ĕsaw more than Ya'aqoḇ because he was the first-born, but now I love Ya'aqoḇ more than Ĕsaw, for Ĕsaw has done many evil deeds, and there is no righteousness in him. All his ways are unrighteousness and violence.

My heart is troubled because of all his deeds. Neither he nor his offspring will be exempt because they are those who will be destroyed from the earth and who will be rooted out from under shamayim. He and his children have forsaken the Aluahim of Aḇraham and gone after his wives (wives' Aluahims) and after their uncleanness and after their error.
You told me to make him swear that he will not kill Ya'aqoḇ his brother, but even if he swears, he will not abide by his oath. He will not do good but evil only.
If he desires to kill Ya'aqoḇ, his brother, then into Ya'aqoḇ's hands he will be given. He will not escape from Ya'aqoḇ's hands.
Do not be afraid for Ya'aqoḇ, for the guardian of Ya'aqoḇ is great, powerful, honored, and praised more than the guardian of Ĕsaw."
Riḇqah called for Ĕsaw and he came to her, and she said to him, "I have a request of you, my son. Promise to do it, my son."
He said, "I will do everything that you say to me, and I will not refuse your request."
She said to him, "I ask you that the day
He said, "1 will do all that you have told me. I shall bury you on the day you die near Sarah, my father's mother, as you have desired that her bones may be near your bones.

Ya'aqoḇ, my brother, I shall love above all flesh. I have only one brother in all the earth but him. It is only what is expected of me. It is no great thing if I love him, for he is my brother, and we were sown together in your body, and together came we out from your womb. If I do not love my brother, whom shall I love?

I beg you to exhort Ya'aqoḇ concerning me and concerning my sons, for I know that he will assuredly be king over me and my sons, for on the day my father baruk him he made him the higher and me the lower.
I swear to you that I shall love him, and not desire evil against him all the days of my life but good only."
And he swore to her regarding all this matter. While Ĕsaw was there, she called Ya'aqoḇ and gave him her orders according to the words that she had spoken to Ĕsaw.
He said, "1 shall do your pleasure, believe me that no evil will proceed from me or from my sons against Ĕsaw. I shall be first in nothing except in love only."

She and her sons ate and drank that night, and she died, three jubilees and one week and one year old on that night. Her two sons, Ĕsaw and Ya'aqoḇ, buried her in the double cave near Sarah, their father's mother. [

In the sixth year of this week Yitsḥaq called his two sons Ĕsaw and Ya'aqoḇ, and they came to him, and he said to them, "My sons, I am going the way of my fathers, to the eternal house where my fathers are.
Bury me near Aḇraham my father, in the double cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite, where Aḇraham purchased a sepulcher to bury in. Bury me in the sepulcher I dug for myself.
I command you, my sons, to practice righteousness and uprightness on the earth, so that Yahuah may do to you what he said he would do to Aḇraham and to his offspring.
Love one another. Love your brothers as a man who loves his own nephesh. Let each seek how he may benefit his brother, and act together on the earth. Let them love each other as their own nepheshoth.
I command and warn you to reject idols. Hate them, and do not love them. They are fully deceptive to those that worship them and for those that bow down to them.

Remember Yahuah Aluahim of Aḇraham, your father, and how I worshipped Him and served Him in righteousness and in joy, that Aluahim might multiply you and increase your offspring as the multitude of stars in shamayim, and establish you on the earth as the plant of righteousness, which will not be rooted out to all the generations forever.

And now I shall make you swear a great oath, for there is no oath which is greater than that which is by the name glorious, honored, great, splendid, wonderful and mighty, which created the shamayim and the earth and all things together, that you will fear Him and worship Him.

Each will love his brother with affection and righteousness. Neither will desire to do evil against his brother from now on forever all the days of your life so that you may prosper in all your deeds and not be destroyed.

If either of you plans evil against his brother, know that he that plans evil shall fall into his brother's hand, and shall be rooted out of the land of the living, and his offspring shall be destroyed from under shamayim.

But on that day there will be turbulence, curses, wrath, anger, and will He burn his land and his city and all that is his with a devouring fire like the fire He sent to burn Sodom and he shall be blotted out of the book of the discipline of the children of men, and he will not be recorded in the book of life. He shall be added in the book of destruction. He shall depart into eternal curses. Their condemnation may be always renewed in hate and in curses and in wrath and in torment and in anger and in plagues and in disease forever.
My sons, this, I say and testify to you, will be the result according to the mishpat which shall come on the man who wishes to injure his brother."

Then he divided all his possessions between the two on that day, and he gave the larger portion to him that was the first-born, and the tower and all that was around it, and all that Aḇraham possessed at the Well of the Oath.
He said, "This larger portion I will give to the first-born."
Ĕsaw said, "I have sold and relinquished my birthright to Ya'aqoḇ. Let it be given to him. I have nothing to say regarding it, for it is his."

Yitsḥaq said, "May a berakah rest on you, my sons, and on your offspring this day. You have given me rest, and my heart is not pained concerning the birthright, or that you should work wickedness because of it.
May the Most High Aluahim bless the man and his offspring forever that does righteousness."

He stopped commanding them and berakah them, and they ate and drank together in front of him, and he rejoiced because there was one mind between them, and they went out from him and rested that day and slept.

Yitsḥaq slept on his bed that day rejoicing. He slept the eternal sleep, and died one hundred and eighty years old. He lived twenty-five weeks and five years; and his two sons, Ĕsaw and Ya'aqoḇ, buried him.
After that Ĕsaw went to the land of Edom, to the mountains of Seir, and lived there.

Ya'aqoḇ lived in the mountains of Hebron, in the high place of the land in which his father Aḇraham had journeyed. He worshipped Yahuah with all his heart. He had divided the days of his generations according to the commands he had seen.

Leah, his wife, died in the fourth year of the second week of the forty­ fifth jubilee, and he buried her in the double cave near Riḇqah his mother to the left of the grave of Sarah, his father's mother. All her sons and his sons came to mourn over Leah, his wife, with him and to comfort him regarding her. He was lamenting her for he loved her greatly after Rachel, her sister, died. She was perfect and upright in all her ways and she honored Ya'aqoḇ. All the days that she lived with him he did not hear from her mouth a harsh word, for she was gentle, peaceable, upright and honorable.
And he remembered all the deeds she had done during her life and he lamented her greatly. He loved her with all his heart and with all his nephesh. [
On the day that Yitsḥaq, the father of Ya'aqoḇ and Ĕsaw died, the sons of Ĕsaw heard that Yitsḥaq had given the elder's portion to his younger son, Ya'aqoḇ, and they were very angry.
They argued with their father, saying, "Why has your father given Ya'aqoḇ the portion of the elder and passed you over even though you are the elder and Ya'aqoḇ the younger?"

He said to them, "Because I sold my birthright to Ya'aqoḇ for a small portion of lentils (lentil soup), and on the day my father sent me to hunt, catch, and bring him something that he should eat and bless me, Ya'aqoḇ came with deceit and brought my father food and drink. My father baruk him and put me under his hand.

Now our father has caused Ya'aqoḇ and me to swear that we shall not devise evil plans against his brother (each other), and that we shall continue in love and in peace each with his brother and not make our ways corrupt."

They said to him, "We shall not listen to you to make peace with him. We are stronger than him and we are more powerful than he is. We shall depose him and kill him, and destroy him and his sons. If you will not go with us, we shall hurt you also.

Listen! Let us send to Aram, Philistia, Moab, and Ammon. Let us take chosen men who are trained in battle, and let us go against him and do battle with him. Let us exterminate him from the earth before he grows strong."
Their father said to them, "Do not go and do not make war with him or you shall fall before him."
They said to him, "This is how you have acted from your youth until this day. You have continued to put your neck under his yoke. We shall not listen to these words."
Then they sent to Aram, and to Aduram to the friend of their father, and they also hired one thousand chosen men of war.

And there came to them from Moab and from the children of Ammon, those who were hired, one thousand chosen men, and from Philistia, one thousand chosen warriors, and from Edom and from the Horites one thousand chosen warriors, and from the Kittim mighty warriors.
They said to their father, "Go out with them and lead them or else we shall kill you."
And he was filled with boiling anger on seeing that his sons were forcing him to go before them to lead them against Ya'aqoḇ, his brother.

But afterward he remembered all the evil that lay hidden in his heart against Ya'aqoḇ his brother, and he did not remember the oath he had sworn to his father and to his mother that he would plan no evil against Ya'aqoḇ, his brother, all his days.

Because Ya'aqoḇ was in mourning for his wife Leah, he did not know they were coming to battle against him until they approached the tower with four thousand soldiers and chosen warriors. The men of Hebron sent to him saying, "Look your brother has come against you to fight. He has with him four thousand men carrying swords, shields, and weapons." They told him this because they loved Ya'aqoḇ more than Ĕsaw.
So they told him, for Ya'aqoḇ was a more gracious and merciful man than Ĕsaw.
But Ya'aqoḇ would not believe until they came very near to the tower.

He closed the gates of the tower; and he stood on the battlements and spoke to his brother Ĕsaw and said, "Noble is the comfort you have come to give me concerning the death of my wife. Is this the oath that you swore to your father and again to your mother before they died? You have broken the oath, and on the moment that you swore to your father you were condemned."

Then Ĕsaw answered and said to him, "Neither the children of men nor the beasts of the earth have sworn an oath of righteousness and kept it forever. Every day they lay evil plans one against another regarding how they might kill their adversary or foe.
You will hate my children and me forever, so there is no observing the tie of brotherhood with you.

Hear these words that I declare to you. If the boar can change its skin and make its bristles as soft as wool, or if it can cause horns to sprout out on its head like the horns of a stag or of a sheep, then will I observe the tie of brotherhood with you. Like breasts separate themselves from their mother (and fight), you and I have never been brothers.

If the wolves make peace with the lambs and not devour or do them violence, and if their hearts are towards them for good, then there shall be peace in my heart towards you. If the lion becomes the friend of the ox and makes peace with him and if he is bound under one yoke with him and plows with him, then will I make peace with you.

When the raven becomes white as the raza (a white bird?), then know that I have loved you and shall make peace with you. You will be rooted out, and your sons shall be rooted out, and there shall be no peace for you."

Ya'aqoḇ saw that Ĕsaw had decided in his heart to do evil toward him, and that he desired with all his nephesh to kill him. Ya'aqoḇ saw that Ĕsaw had come pouncing like the wild boar which charges the spear that is set to pierce and kill it, and yet does not even slow down. Then he spoke to his own people and to his servants and told them that Ĕsaw and his men were going to attack him and all his companions. [

After that Yahuḏah spoke to Ya'aqoḇ, his father, and said to him, "Bend your bow, father, and send forth your arrows and bring down the adversary and kill the enemy. You have the power to do it. We will not kill your brother because he is your kin and he is like you, so we will honor his life."
Then Ya'aqoḇ bent his bow and sent forth the arrow and struck Ĕsaw, his brother, on the right side of his chest and killed him.

And again he sent forth an arrow and struck Adoran the Aramaean, on the left side of his chest, and it drove him backward and killed him. Then the sons of Ya'aqoḇ and their servants went out, dividing themselves into companies on the four sides of the tower.
Yahuḏah went out in front. Naphtali and Gad along with fifty servants went to the south side of the tower, and they killed all they found before them. Not one individual escaped.
Lewi, Dan, and Asher went out on the east side of the tower along with fifty men, and they killed the warriors of Moab and Ammon.
Re'uḇen, Issachar, and Zebulon went out on the north side of the tower along with fifty men and they killed the warriors of the Philistines.

Re'uḇen's son, Shim'on, Binyamin, and Ḥanoḵ went out on the west side of the tower along with fifty men and they killed four hundred men, stout warriors of Edom and of the Horites. Six hundred fled, and four of the sons of Ĕsaw fled with them, and left their father lying killed, as he had fallen on the hill that is in Aduram.
And the sons of Ya'aqoḇ pursued them to the mountains of Seir. And Ya'aqoḇ buried his brother on the hill that is in Aduram, and he returned to his house.
The sons of Ya'aqoḇ crushed the sons of Ĕsaw in the mountains of Seir, and made them bow their necks so that they became servants of the sons of Ya'aqoḇ.
They sent a message to their father to inquire whether they should make peace with them or kill them.
Ya'aqoḇ sent word to his sons that they should make peace. They made peace with them but also placed the yoke of servitude on them, so that they paid tribute to Ya'aqoḇ and to his sons always.
And they continued to pay tribute to Ya'aqoḇ until the day that he went down to Mitsrayim.
The sons of Edom have not escaped the yoke of servitude imposed by the twelve sons of Ya'aqoḇ until this day.
These are the kings that reigned in Edom before there was any king over the children of Yasharal (until this day) in the land of Edom.
And Balaq, the son of Beor, reigned in Edom, and the name of his city was Danaba. Balaq died, and Jobab, the son of Zara of Boser, ruled in his place.
Jobab died, and Asam, of the land of Ternan, ruled in his place.
Asam died, and Adath, the son of Barad, who killed Miḏyan in the field of Moab, ruled in his place, and the name of his city was Avith.
Adath died, and Salman, from Amaseqa, ruled in his place.
Salman died, and Saul of Ra'aboth by the river, ruled in his place. Saul died, and Ba' elunan, the son of Achbor, ruled in his place.

Ba' elunan, the son of Achbor died, and Adath ruled in his place, and the name of his wife was Maitabith, the daughter of Matarat, the daughter of Metabedza' abo These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom. [
Ya'aqoḇ lived in the land that his father journeyed in, which is the land of Kena'an.
These are the generations of Ya'aqoḇ. Yoseph was seventeen years old when they took him down into the land of Mitsrayim, and Potiphar, a eunuch of Pharaoh, the chief cook, bought him.
He made Yoseph the manager over Potiphar's entire house and the berakah of Yahuah came on the house of the Mitsrite because of Yoseph. And Yahuah caused him to prosper in all that he did.
The Mitsrite turned everything over to the hands of Yoseph because he saw that Yahuah was with him, and that Yahuah caused him to prosper him in all that he did.
Yoseph's appearance was beautiful, and his master's wife watched Yoseph, and she loved him and wanted him to have sex with her.

But he did not surrender his nephesh because he remembered Yahuah and the words which Ya'aqoḇ, his father, used to read to him from the writings of Aḇraham, that no man should commit fornication with a woman who has a husband. For him the punishment of death has been ordained in the shamayim before the Most High Aluahim, and the sin will be recorded against him in the eternal books, which are always in the presence of Yahuah.
Yoseph remembered these words and refused to have sex with her.
And she begged him for a year, but he refused and would not listen.

But while he was in the house she embraced him and held him tightly in order to force him to sleep with her. She closed the doors of the house and held on to him, but he left his garment in her hands and broke through the door and ran out from her presence.

The woman saw that he would not sleep with her, and she slandered him in the presence of his master, saying "Your Hebrew servant, whom you love, sought to force me to have sex with him. When I shouted for help he fled and left his garment in my hands. I tried to stop him but he broke through the door."
When the Mitsrite saw Yoseph's garment and the broken door, and heard the words of his wife, he threw Yoseph into prison and put him in the place where the prisoners of the king were kept.

He was there in the prison, and Yahuah gave Yoseph favor in the sight of the chief of the prison guards and caused him to have compassion for Yoseph, because he saw that Yahuah was with him, and that Yahuah made all that he did to prosper.

He turned over all things into his hands, and the chief of the prison guards knew of nothing that was going on in the prison, because Yoseph did everything for him, and Yahuah perfected it. He remained there two years.

In those days Pharaoh, king of Mitsrayim, was very angry at his two eunuchs, the chief butler, and the chief baker. He put them in the prison facility of the house of the chief cook, where Yoseph was kept.
The chief of the prison guards appointed Yoseph to serve them, and he served them.
They both dreamed a dream, the chief butler and the chief baker, and they told it to Yoseph.
As he interpreted to them so it happened to them, and Pharaoh restored the chief butler to his office and he killed the chief baker as Yoseph had interpreted to them.

But the chief butler forgot Yoseph was in the prison, although he had informed him of what would happen to him. He did not remember to inform Pharaoh of how Yoseph had told him (about his dream), because he forgot. [

In those days Pharaoh dreamed two dreams in one night concerning a famine that was to be in all the land, and he awoke from his sleep and called all the magicians and interpreters of dreams that were in Mitsrayim. He told them his two dreams but they were not able to tell him what they meant.
Then the chief butler remembered Yoseph and told the king of him, and he brought him out from the prison, and the king told his two dreams to him.

He said before Pharaoh that his two dreams were one, and he said to him, "Seven years shall come in which there shall be plenty in all the land of Mitsrayim, but after that, seven years of famine. Such a famine as has not been in all the land.

Now, let Pharaoh appoint administrators in all the land of Mitsrayim, and let them store up food in every city throughout all the years of plenty, and there will be food for the seven years of famine, and those of the land will not perish through the famine, even though it will be very severe."
Yahuah gave Yoseph favor and mercy in the eyes of Pharaoh. Pharaoh said to his servants, "We shall not find such a wise and prudent man like this man, because the spirit of Yahuah is with him."
And he appointed Yoseph the second in command in his entire kingdom and gave him authority over all Mitsrayim, and placed him on the second chariot of Pharaoh to ride.

And he clothed him with fine linen clothes, and he put a gold chain around his neck, and a crier proclaimed before him "EI" "EI wa Abirer," and he placed a ring on his hand and made him ruler over all his house, and lifted him up before the people, and said to him, "Only on the throne shall I be greater than you."

Yoseph ruled over all the land of Mitsrayim, and all the governors of Pharaoh, and all his servants, and all those who did the king's business loved him because he walked in uprightness, because he was without pride and arrogance. He did not judge people by their position, and did not accept gifts, but he judged all the people of the land in uprightness.

The land of Mitsrayim was at peace before Pharaoh because of Yoseph, because Yahuah was with him, and Yahuah gave him favor and mercy for all his generations before all those who knew him and those who heard of him, and Pharaoh's kingdom was run efficiently, and there was no Satan (adversary) and no evil person in it.
And the king called Yoseph's name Sephantiphans, and gave Yoseph the daughter of Potiphar, the daughter of the kohen of Heliopolis, the chief cook to marry.
On the day that Yoseph stood before Pharaoh he was thirty years old.

In that year Yitsḥaq died. Things transpired as Yoseph had said in the interpretation of Pharaoh's dream and there were seven years of plenty over all the land of Mitsrayim, and the land of Mitsrayim abundantly produced, one measure producing eighteen hundred measures.
Yoseph gathered food into every city until they were full of grain and they could no longer count or measure it because of its multitude. [
In the forty-fifth jubilee, in the second week, and in the second year, Yahuḏah took his first-born Er, a wife from the daughters of Aram, named Tamar.

But he hated her, and did not have sex with her, because her mother was of the daughters of Kena'an, and he wished to take him a wife of the lineage of his mother, but Yahuḏah, his father, would not permit him to do that.
Er, the first-born of Yahuḏah, was wicked, and Yahuah killed him.
And Yahuḏah said to Onan, his brother, "Go in to your brother's wife and perform the duty of a husband's brother to her, and raise up offspring to your brother."

Onan knew that the offspring would not be his, but his brother's only, and he went into the house of his brother's wife, and spilt his seed (ejaculates) on the ground, and he was wicked in the eyes of Yahuah, and He killed him.
Yahuḏah said to Tamar, his daughter-in-law, "Remain in your father's house as a widow until Shelah, my son has grown up, and I shall give you to him to wife."
He grew up, but Bedsu' el, the wife of Yahuḏah, did not permit her son Shelah to marry. Bedsu'el, Yahuḏah's wife, died in the fifth year of this week.
In the sixth year Yahuḏah went up to shear his sheep at Timnah.

And they told Tamar, "Look, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep." And she took off her widow's clothes, and put on a veil, and adorned herself, and sat in the gate connecting the road to Timnah.

As Yahuḏah was going along he saw her, and thought she was a prostitute, and he said to her, "Let me come in to you," and she said to him, "Come in," and he went in. n She said to him, "Give me my pay," and he said to her, "I have nothing with me except my ring that is on my finger, my necklace, and my staff which is in my hand."
She said to him, "Give them to me until you send me my pay." And he said to her, "I will send to you a kid of the goats", and he gave her his ring, necklace, and staff, and she conceived by him.
Yahuḏah went to his sheep, and she went to her father's house.
Yahuḏah sent a kid of the goats by the hand of his shepherd, an Adullamite, but he could not find her, so he asked the people of the place, saying, "Where is the prostitute who was here?"

They said to him, "There is no prostitute here with us." And he returned and informed Yahuḏah that he had not found her, "I asked the people of the place, and they said to me, "There is no prostitute here." "

He said, "If you see her give the kids to her or we become a cause of ridicule." And when she had completed three months, it was revealed that she was with child, and they told Yahuḏah, saying, "Look Tamar, your daughter-in-law, is with child by whoredom."
And Yahuḏah went to the house of her father, and said to her father and her brothers, "Bring her out, and let them burn her, for she has committed uncleanness in Yasharal."
It happened when they brought her out to burn her that she sent to her father-in-law the ring and the necklace, and the staff, saying, "Tell us whose are these, because by him am I with child."
Yahuḏah acknowledged, and said, "Tamar is more righteous than I am.

Do not let them burn her." For that reason she was not given to Shelah, and he did not again approach her and after that she gave birth to two sons, Perez and Zerah, in the seventh year of this second week.
At this time the seven years of fruitfulness were completed, of which Yoseph spoke to Pharaoh.
Yahuḏah acknowledged the evil deed that he had done because he had sex with his daughter-in-law, and he hated himself for it.
He acknowledged that he had transgressed and gone astray, because he had uncovered the skirt of his son, and he began to lament and to supp licate before Yahuah because of his transgression.
We told him in a dream that it was forgiven him because he supplicated earnestly, and lamented, and did not commit the act again.

And he received forgiveness because he turned from his sin and from his ignorance, because he transgressed greatly before our Aluahim. Every one that acts like this, every one who has sex with his mother-in-law, let them burn him alive with fire. Because there is uncleanness and pollution on them, let them burn them alive.

Command the children of Yasharal that there should be no uncleanness among them, because every one who has sex with his daughter-in-law or with his mother-in-law has committed uncleanness. Let them burn the man who has had sex with her with fire, and likewise burn the woman, so that Aluahim will turn away wrath and punishment from Yasharal.
We told Yahuḏah that his two sons had not had sex with her, and for this reason his offspring was established for a second generation, and would not be rooted out.
For in single-mindedness he had gone and sought for punishment, namely, according to the mishpat of Aḇraham, which he had commanded his sons. Yahuḏah had sought to burn her alive. [
In the first year of the third week of the forty-fifth jubilee the famine began to come into the land, and the rain refused to be given to the earth. None whatsoever fell.
The earth became barren, but in the land of Mitsrayim there was food, because Yoseph had gathered the seed of the land in the seven years of plenty and had preserved it.
The Mitsrites came to Yoseph that he might give them food, and he opened the storehouses where the grain of the first year was stored, and he sold it to the people of the land for gold.
Ya'aqoḇ heard there was food in Mitsrayim, and he sent his ten sons that they should procure food for him in Mitsrayim, and they arrived among those that went there, but Binyamin he did not send.
Yoseph recognized them, but they did not recognize him. He spoke to them and questioned them, and he said to them, "Are you not spies and have you not come to explore ways to enter this land?"
And he put them in custody.
After that, he set them free again, and detained Shim'on alone and sent his nine brothers away.

He filled their sacks with corn, and he put their gold back in their sacks, and they did not know it. Yoseph then commanded them to bring their younger brother, because they had told him their father was living and also their younger brother.

They went up from the land of Mitsrayim and they came to the land of Kena'an. There they told their father all that had happened to them, and how the ruler of the country had spoken rudely to them, and had seized Shim'on until they should bring Binyamin.
Ya'aqoḇ said, "You have taken my children from me! Yoseph is gone and Shim'on also is gone, and now you will take Binyamin away. I am the victim of your wickedness."

He said, "My son will not go down with you because fate may have it that he would fall sick. Their mother gave birth to two sons, and one has died, and this one also you will take from me. If, by fate, he took a fever on the road, you would turn my oId age to sorrow and death."
He saw that every man's money had been returned to him in his sack, and for this reason he feared to send him.

The famine increased and became grievous in the land of Kena'an, and in all lands except in the land of Mitsrayim. Mitsrayim had food because many of the children of the Mitsrites had stored up their seed for food from the time when they saw Yoseph gathering seed together and putting it in storehouses and preserving it for the years of famine.

The people of Mitsrayim fed themselves on it during the first year of their famine but when Yasharal saw that the famine was very serious in the land, and that there was no deliverance, he said to his sons, "Go again, and procure food for us so that we will not die."
They said, "We shall not go unless our youngest brother go with us! "
Yasharal saw that if he did not send Binyamin with them, they would all perish because of the famine.
Re'uḇen said, "Give him to me, and if I do not bring him back to you, kill my two sons in payment for his nephesh." Yasharal said to Re'uḇen, "He shall not go with you."
Yahuḏah came near and said, "Send him with me, and if I do not bring him back to you, let me bear your blame all the days of my life," Yoseph 8. Lumpkin
He sent him with them in the second year of this week on the first day of the month.
They all came to the land of Mitsrayim, and they had presents in their hands of sweet spice, almonds, turpentine nuts, and pure honey.
And they went and stood before Yoseph, and he saw Binyamin his brother, and he knew him, and said to them, "Is this your youngest brother?" They said to him, "It is he."

He said, "Yahuah be gracious to you, my son!" And he sent Binyamin into his house and he brought out Shim'on to them. Yoseph made a feast for them, and they presented to him the gifts that they had brought in their hands.
They ate before Yoseph and he gave them all a portion of food, but the portion of food given to Binyamin was seven times larger than any of theirs.
And they ate and drank and got up and remained with their donkeys.

Yoseph devised a plan whereby he might learn their thoughts as to whether they desired peace or not. He said to the steward who was over his house, "Fill all their sacks with food. Place their money back in their vessels. Put my cup, the silver cup out of which I drink, in the sack of the youngest and send them away." [
He did as Yoseph had told him, and filled all their sacks with food for them and put their money back into their sacks, and put the cup in Binyamin's sack.

Early in the morning they departed, and it happened that when they had gone from that place, Yoseph said to the steward of his house, "Pursue them, run and seize them, and say, 'You have repaid my kindness with evil. You have stolen from me the silver cup out of which my Yahuah drinks.'
Bring me back their youngest brother. Go! Get him quickly before I go to my seat of mishpat (judge you guilt of disobeying an order). "

He ran after them and said the words as he was told. They said to him, "Aluahim forbid that your servants should do this thing, and steal any utensil or money from the house of your Yahuah, like the things we found in our sacks the first time we, your servants, came back from the land of Kena'an.

We have not stolen any utensil. How could we? Look here in our sacks and search, and wherever you find the cup in the sack of any man among us, let him be killed, and we and our donkeys will serve your Yahuah."
He said to them, "Not so. If I find it, the man whose sack I find it in I shall take as a servant, and the rest of you shall return in peace to your house."
He was searching in their vessels, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest, when it was found in Binyamin's sack.

They ripped their garments in frustration, and placed their belongings back on their donkeys, and returned to the city and came to the house of Yoseph. They all bowed themselves with their faces to the ground in front of him.

Yoseph said to them, "You have done evil." They said, "What shall we say and how shall we defend ourselves? Our Yahuah has discovered the transgression of his servants; and now we and our donkeys are the servants of our Yahuah."

Yoseph said to them, "I too fear Yahuah. As for you, go to your homes and let your brother be my servant, because you have done evil. I delight in this cup as no one else delights in his cup and yet you have stolen it from me."

Yahuḏah said, "a my Yahuah, I pray you to let your servant speak a word in my Yahuah's ear. Your servant's mother had two sons for our father. One went away and was lost, and has not been found since. This one alone is left of his mother, and your servant our father loves him. He would die if the lad were lost to him.
When we go to your servant our father, and the lad is not with us, it will happen that he will die. We will have brought so much sorrow on our father it will bring his death.

Now rather let me, your servant, stay here as a bondsman to my Yahuah instead of the boy. Let the lad go with his brothers, because I will stand in for him at the hand of your servant our father. If I do not bring him back, your servant will bear the blame of our father forever."
Yoseph saw that they were all in accord in doing good to one another. Then, he could not refrain himself, and he told them that he was Yoseph.
And he conversed with them in the Hebrew tongue and hugged their necks and wept.

At first they did not recognize him and then they began to weep. He said to them, "Do not weep for me, but hurry and bring my father to me. See, it is my mouth that speaks and the eyes of my brother Binyamin see me.
Pay attention. This is the second year of the famine, and there are still five years to come without harvest or fruit of trees or plowing.

You and your households come down quickly, so that you won't die because of the famine. Do not be grieved for your possessions, because Yahuah sent me before you to set things in order that many people might live.
Tell my father that I am still alive. You see that Yahuah has made me as a father to Pharaoh, and ruler over his house and over all the land of Mitsrayim.
Tell my father of all my glory, and all the riches and glory that Yahuah has given me."
By the command of Pharaoh's mouth, he gave them chariots and provisions for the way, and he gave them all multi-colored raiment and silver.
He sent corn, raiment, silver, and ten donkeys that carried all of this to his father, and he sent them away.
They went up and told their father that Yoseph was alive, and was measuring out corn to all the nations of the earth, and that he was ruler over all the land of Mitsrayim.

But their father did not believe it, because he was not in his right mind. But when he saw the wagons, which Yoseph had sent, the life of his spirit revived, and he said, "It is enough for me if Yoseph lives. I will go down and see him before I die." [

Yasharal took his journey from Haran's house on the new moon of the third month, and he stopped at the Well of the Oath on the way and he offered a sacrifice to the Aluahim of his father Yitsḥaq on the seventh of this month.
Ya'aqoḇ remembered the dream that he had at Bethel, and he feared to go down into Mitsrayim.

He was thinking of sending word to Yoseph to come to him because he did not want to go down. He remained there seven days, hoping fate would permit him to see a vision as to whether he should remain or go down.

He celebrated the harvest festival of the first-fruits with old grain, because in all the land of Kena'an there was not a handful of seed in the ground because the famine was affecting all the beasts, and cattle, and birds, and all men. S On the sixteenth Yahuah appeared to him, and said to him, "Ya'aqoḇ, Ya'aqoḇ," and he said, "Here I am."

And He said to him, "I am the Aluahim of your fathers, the Aluahim of Aḇraham and Yitsḥaq. Do not be afraid to go down into Mitsrayim, because I will be there to make you a great nation. I will go down with you, and I will bring you up again. You will be buried in this land and Yoseph will put his hands on your eyes (to close them in death). Do not be afraid. Go down into Mitsrayim."
And his sons got up and placed their father and their possessions on wagons.
Yasharal got up from the Well of the Oath on the sixteenth of this third month, and he went to the land of Mitsrayim.
Yasharal sent Yahuḏah before him to his son Yoseph to examine the Land of Goshen, because Yoseph had told his brothers that they should come and live there so they could be near him.
This was the best land in Mitsrayim. It was near to him and suitable for all of the cattle they had.
These are the names of the sons of Ya'aqoḇ who went into Mitsrayim with Ya'aqoḇ their father; Re'uḇen, the First-born of Yasharal and his sons Ḥanoḵ, and Pallu, and Hezron and Carmi, making five.
Shim'on and his sons Jemuel, and Jamin, and Ohad, and Jachin, and Zohar, and Shaul, the son of the Zephathite woman, making seven.
Lewi and his sons Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari, making four.
Yahuḏah and his sons Shela, and Perez, and Zerah, making four.
Issachar and his sons Tola, and Phua, and Jasub, and Shimron, making five.
Zebulon and his sons Sered, and Elon, and Jahleel, making four.

These are the sons of Ya'aqoḇ and their sons whom Leah bore to Ya'aqoḇ in Mesopotamia, six, and their one sister, Dinah and all the nepheshoth of the sons of Leah, and their sons, who went with Ya'aqoḇ their father into Mitsrayim. Twenty­ nine nepheshoth, and Ya'aqoḇ, making thirty, were the number of people that went into Mitsrayim.
And the sons of Zilpah, Leah's handmaid, the wife of Ya'aqoḇ, who bore to Ya'aqoḇ Gad and Ashur and their sons who went with him into Mitsrayim.

The sons of Gad are Ziphion, and Haggi, and Shuni, and Ezbon, and Eri, and Areli, and Arodi, which make eight nepheshoth in total. The sons of Asher are Imnah, and Ishvah, and Ishvi, and Beriah, and Serah, and their one sister, which makes six in total.
All the nepheshoth were fourteen, and all those of Leah were forty-four.
The sons of Rachel, the wife of Ya'aqoḇ are Yoseph and Binyamin.

There were born to Yoseph in Mitsrayim before his father came into Mitsrayim, those whom Asenath, daughter of Potiphar, kohen of Heliopolis gave birth to him, Menashsheh, and Ephrayim. The wife and children of Yoseph totaled three.
The sons of Binyamin, Bela and Becher and Ashbel, Gera, and Naaman, and Ehi, and Rosh, and Muppim, and Huppim, and Ard with Binyamin totaled eleven.
And all the nepheshoth of Rachel were fourteen.
And the sons of Bilhah, the handmaid of Rachel, the wife of Ya'aqoḇ, whom she gave birth to Ya'aqoḇ, were Dan and Naphtali. These are the names of their sons who went with them into Mitsrayim.
The sons of Dan were Hushim, and Samon, and Asudi. and "Ijaka, and Salomon, all totaling six.
All but one died the year in which they entered into Mitsrayim, and there was left to Dan only Hushim.
These are the names of the sons of Naphtali: Jahziel, and Guni and Jezer, and Shallum, and 'Iv.
And 'Iv, who was born after the years of famine, died in Mitsrayim.
All the nepheshoth (offspring) of Rachel were twenty-six.
All the nepheshoth (offspring) of Ya'aqoḇ, which went into Mitsrayim, were seventy nepheshoth.
These are his children and his children's children, in all seventy, but five died in Mitsrayim in the time of Yoseph's rule and they had no children.
In the land of Kena'an two sons of Yahuḏah died, Er and Onan, and they had no children, and the children of Yasharal buried those who died, and they were counted among the seventy Gentile nations. [
On the new moon of the fourth month, in the second year of the third week of the forty-fifth jubilee, Yasharal went into the country of Mitsrayim, to the land of Goshen.
Yoseph went to meet his father, Ya'aqoḇ, in the land of Goshen, and he hugged his father's neck and wept.

Yasharal said to Yoseph, "Now that I have seen you let me die and may Yahuah Aluahim of Yasharal, the Aluahim of Aḇraham, and the Aluahim of Yitsḥaq, who has not withheld His mercy and His grace from His servant Ya'aqoḇ, be baruk.
It is enough for me to have seen your face while I am yet alive. Yes, this is the true vision which I saw at Bethel.
Baruk be Yahuah my Aluahim forever and ever, and baruk be His name./I

Yoseph and his brothers ate bread in the presence of their father and drank wine, and Ya'aqoḇ rejoiced with very great joy because he saw Yoseph eating with his brothers and drinking in the presence of him, and he baruk the Creator of all things who had preserved him, and had preserved for him his twelve sons.
Yoseph had given his father and his brothers as a gift the right of dwelling in the land of Goshen and in Rameses and the entire region around it, which he ruled over in the presence of Pharaoh.

Yasharal and his sons dwelt in the land of Goshen, the best part of the land of Mitsrayim, and Yasharal was one hundred and thirty years old when he came into Mitsrayim. Yoseph nourished his father and his brothers and also their possessions (servants) with bread as much as they needed for the seven years of the famine.

The land of Mitsrayim became available for purchase because of the famine, and Yoseph acquired all the land of Mitsrayim for Pharaoh in return for food, and he got possession of the people and their cattle and everything for Pharaoh.
