The years of the famine were completed, and Yoseph gave the people in the land seed and food that they might sow the land in the eighth year, because the river had overflowed all the land of Mitsrayim.

For in the seven years of the famine it had not overflowed and had irrigated only a few places on the banks of the river, but now it overflowed and the Mitsrites sowed the land, and it produced much corn that year.

This was the first year of the fourth week of the forty-fifth jubilee. Yoseph took one-fifth of the corn of the harvest for the king and left four parts for them for food and for seed, and Yoseph made it a law for Mitsrayim until this day.

Yasharal lived in the land of Mitsrayim seventeen years, and all the days which he lived were three jubilees, one hundred and forty-seven years, and he died in the fourth year of the fifth week of the forty-fifth jubilee.

Yasharal baruk his sons before he died and told them everything that they would go through in the land of Mitsrayim. He revealed to them what they would live through in the last days, and he baruk them and gave Yoseph two portions of the land.
He slept with his fathers, and he was buried in the double cave in the land of Kena'an, near Aḇraham his father, in the grave which he dug for himself in the land of Hebron.
And he gave all his books and the books of his fathers to Lewi, his son so that he might preserve them and replicate them for his children until this day. [

There was neither Satan nor any evil in all the days of the life of Yoseph after his father, Ya'aqoḇ (had died), because all the Mitsrites respected the children of Yasharal all the days of the life of Yoseph.

Yoseph died, being a hundred and ten years old. He lived seventeen years in the land of Kena'an, and ten years he was a servant, and three years in prison, and eighty years he was under the king, ruling all the land of Mitsrayim.

He died and so did all his brothers and all of that generation. But, he commanded the children of Yasharal before he died that they should carry his bones with them when they went out from the land of Mitsrayim.

And he made them swear regarding his bones, because he knew that the Mitsrites would not bring his bones out of Mitsrayim or bury him in the land of Kena'an, because while dwelling in the land of Assyria, king Makamaron, the king of Kena'an, fought against Mitsrayim in the valley and killed the king of Mitsrayim there, and pursued the Mitsrites to the gates of "Ermon.

But he was not able to enter, because another king, a new king, had become king of Mitsrayim, and he was stronger than he (Makamaron), and he returned to the land of Kena'an, and the gates of Mitsrayim were closed so that none came or went from Mitsrayim.
Yoseph died in the forty-sixth jubilee, in the sixth week, in the second year, and they buried him in the land of Mitsrayim, and all his brothers died after him.

The king of Mitsrayim went to war against the king of Kena'an in the forty­ seventh jubilee, in the second week in the second year, and the children of Yasharal brought out all the bones of the children of Ya'aqoḇ except the bones of Yoseph, and they buried them in the field in the double cave in the mountain.
Then, most of them returned to Mitsrayim, but a few of them remained in the mountains of Hebron, and Amram your father remained with them.
The king of Kena'an was victorious over the king of Mitsrayim, and he closed the gates of Mitsrayim.
He devised an evil plan against the children of Yasharal to afflict them. He said to the people of Mitsrayim, "Look, the people of the children of Yasharal have increased and multiplied more than we.

Let us use wisdom and deal with them before they become too many. Let us make them our slaves before we go to war and they rise up against us on the side of our enemies. Before they leave and fight against us let us do this because their hearts and faces (allegiances) are towards the land of Kena'an."

He set over them taskmasters to enforce slavery, and they built strong cities for Pharaoh, Pithom, and Raamses and they built all the walls and all the fortifications, which had fallen in the cities of Mitsrayim.
They enslaved them with harshness, and the more they were evil toward them, the more they increased and multiplied.
And the people of Mitsrayim despised the children of Yasharal. [

In the seventh week, in the seventh year, in the forty-seventh jubilee, your father went out from the land of Kena'an, and you (Mosheh) were born in the fourth week, in the sixth year of it, in the forty-eighth jubilee; this was the time of tribulation for the children of Yasharal.
Pharaoh, the king of Mitsrayim, issued a command ordering them to throw all their newborn male children into the river.
And they threw them into the river for seven months until the day that you were born. It is said that your mother hid you for three months.

She made an ark for you, and covered it with pitch and tar, and placed it in the reeds on the bank of the river. She placed you in it seven days. Your mother came by night and nursed you. By day Miriam, your sister, guarded you from the birds.
In those days Tharmuth, the daughter of Pharaoh, came to bathe in the river, and she heard you crying. She told her maids to bring you out, and they brought you to her.
She took you out of the ark, and she had compassion on you.
Your sister said to her, "Shall I go and call to you one of the Hebrew women to nurse this baby for you?" And she said to her, "Ga."
Your sister went and called your mother, Yokeḇeḏ, and Pharaoh's daughter gave her wages (employed her), and she nursed you.

Afterwards, when you grew up, they brought you to the daughter of Pharaoh, and you became her son. Amram, your father, taught you writing. After you had completed three weeks (twenty-seven years) they brought you into the royal court.

You were three weeks of years at court until the time when you went out from the royal court and saw an Mitsrite beating your friend who was of the children of Yasharal, and you killed him and hid him in the sand.
On the second day you came across two children of Yasharal quarreling together, and you asked the one who was doing wrong, "Why did you hit your brother?"
He was angry and indignant, and said, "Who made you a prince and a judge over us?
Do you want to kill me like you killed the Mitsrite yesterday?" You were afraid and you fled on because of these words. [

In the sixth year of the third week of the forty-ninth jubilee you fled and went to live in the land of Miḏyan for five weeks and one year. You returned to Mitsrayim in the second week in the second year in the fiftieth jubilee.
You know what He said to you on Mount Sinai, and what prince Mastema desired to do with you when you returned to Mitsrayim.
Did he (Mastema) not seek to kill you with all his power and to deliver the Mitsrites from your hand when he saw that you were sent to execute mishpat and to take revenge on the Mitsrites?

But I delivered you out of his hand, and you performed the signs and wonders which you were sent to perform in Mitsrayim against Pharaoh, and against all of his household, and against his servants and his people. S Yahuah exacted a great vengeance on them for Yasharal's sake, and struck them through the plagues of blood, frogs, lice, dog-flies, malignant boils, breaking out in pustules, the death of their cattle, and the plague of hailstones. He destroyed everything that grew from them by plagues of locusts, which devoured the remainder left by the hail, and by darkness, and by the death of the first-born of men and animals. Yahuah took vengeance on all of their idols and burned them with fire.

Everything was sent through your hand, that you should declare these things before they were done. You spoke with the king of Mitsrayim in the presence of all his servants and in the presence of his people and everything took place according to your words. Ten great and terrible ju dgments came on the land of Mitsrayim so that you might execute vengeance on Mitsrayim for Yasharal.

And Yahuah did everything for Yasharal's sake according to His berith, which he had ordained with Aḇraham. He took vengeance on them because they had brought them by force into bondage. S Prince Mastema stood against you, and sought to deliver you into the hands of Pharaoh. He helped the Mitsrite sorcerers when they stood up and comm itted the evil acts they did in your presence. Indeed, we permitted them to work, but the remedies we did not allow to be worked by their hands.
Yahuah struck them with malignant ulcers (hemorrhoids?), and they were not able to stand. They could not perform a single sign because we destroyed them.

Even after all of these signs and wonders, prince Mastema was not put to shame because he took courage and cried to the Mitsrites to pursue you with all the power the Mitsrites had, with their chariots, and with their horses, and with all the hosts of the peopl es of Mitsrayim.
But I stood between the Mitsrites and Yasharal, and we delivered Yasharal out of his hand, and out of the hand of his people. Yahuah brought them through the middle of the sea as if it were dry land.

Yahuah our Aluahim threw all the people whom he (Mas tema) brought to pursue Yasharal into the middle of the sea, into the depths of the bottomless pit, beneath the children of Yasharal, even as the people of Mitsrayim had thrown their (Yasharal's) children into the river. He took vengeance on one million of them. In add ition to one thousand strong and energetic men were destroyed because of the death of the suckling children of your people, which they had thrown into the river.

On the fourteenth day and on the fifteenth and on the sixteenth and on the seventeenth and on the eighteenth days, prince Mastema was bound and imprisoned and placed behind the children of Yasharal so that he might not accuse them.
On the nineteenth day we let them (Mastema and his demons) loose so that they might help the Mitsrites pursue the children of Yasharal.
He hardened their hearts and made them stubborn, and the plan was devised by Yahuah our Aluahim that He might strike the Mitsrites and throw them into the sea.

On the fourteenth day we bound him that he might not accuse the children of Yasharal on the day when they asked the Mitsrites for vessels and garments, vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and vessels of bronze, in order to exact from the Mitsrites a price in return for the bondage they had been forced to serve.
We did not lead the children of Yasharal from Mitsrayim empty handed. [

Remember the mitsvah which Yahuah commanded you concerning the Pesach. You should celebrate it in its season on the fourteenth day of the first month. You should kill the sacrifice before evening. They should eat it by night on the evening of the fifteenth from the time of the setting of the sun.

Because on this night, at the beginning of the festival and the beginning of the joy, you were eating the Pesach (lamb) in Mitsrayim, when all the powers of Mastema had been let loose to kill all the first-born in the land of Mitsrayim, from the first-born of Pharaoh to the first-born of the captive maid-servant in the mill, and even the first-born of the cattle.

This is the sign that Yahuah gave them, in every house on the door post on which they saw the blood of a lamb of the first year they should not enter to kill, but should pass by it, that all those should be exempt that were in the house because the sign of the blood was on its door posts.
And the powers of Yahuah did everything as Yahuah commanded them, and they passed by all the children of Yasharal, and the plague did not come on them to destroy them, cattle, man, or dog.
The plague was oppressive in Mitsrayim, and there was no house in Mitsrayim where there was not one dead, and weeping, and lamentation.

All Yasharal was eating the flesh of the paschal lamb, and drinking the wine, and was praising, and berakah, and giving thanks to Yahuah Aluahim of their fathers, and they were ready to get out from under the yoke of Mitsrayim and the evil bondage.

Remember this day all the days of your life. Observe it from year to year all the days of your life, once a year, on its day, according to all the law of it. Do not forsake it from day to day, or from month to month.

It is an eternal law, and engraved on the heavenly tablets regarding all the children of Yasharal that they should observe it on its day once a year, every year, throughout all their generations. There is no limit of days, for this is a law forever.

The man who is free from uncleanness, and does not come to observe Pesach on the occasion of its day and does not bring an acceptable offering before Yahuah to eat and to drink before Yahuah on the day of its festival will be guilty. If he is clean and close at hand (near the heykal) and does not come, he shall be cut off because he did not offer the offering of Yahuah in its appointed season. He shall take the guilt on himself.

Let the children of Yasharal come and observe the Pesach on the day of its fixed time, on the fourteenth day of the first month, between the evenings, from the third part of the day to the third part of the night, for two portions of the day are given to the light, and a third part to the evening.
Yahuah commanded you to observe it between the evenings.

And it is not permissible to kill the sacrifice during any period of light, but only during the period bordering on the evening, and let them eat it at the time of the evening, until the third part of the night. Whatever is left over of all its flesh from the third part of the night and onwards is to be burned with fire.

They shall not cook it with water (boil or seethe it), nor shall they eat it raw, but roast it on the fire. They shall eat it with care, making sure its head with the inwards and its feet are roasted with fire, and they shall not break any bone of it, for of the children of Yasharal no bone shall be crushed.

For this reason Yahuah commanded the children of Yasharal to observe the Pesach on the day of its fixed time, and they shall not break a bone of it, because it is a festival day He commanded. There was no passing over from any other day or any other month, but on the exact day let the festival be observed.

Command the children of Yasharal to observe the Pesach throughout their days, every year, once a year on the day of its fixed time, and it shall be a memorial well pleasing in the presence of Yahuah, and no plague shall come on them to kill or to strike in that year in which they celebrate the Pesach in its season in every respect accordi ng to His command.
And they shall not eat it outside the sanctuary of Yahuah, but before the sanctuary of Yahuah, and all the people of the qahal of Yasharal shall celebrate it in its appointed season.

Every man twenty years of age and upward, who has come on the day of the Pesach shall eat it in the sanctuary of your Aluahim before Yahuah. This is how it is written and ordained. They should eat it in the sanctuary of Yahuah.

When the children of Yasharal come into the land of Kena'an that they are to possess, set up the tabernacle (tent) of Yahuah within the land occupied by one of their tribes until the sanctuary of Yahuah has been built in the land. There, let them come and celebrate the Pesach at tabernacle of Yahuah, and let them kill it before Yahuah from year to year.
When the house of Yahuah has been built in the land of their inheritance, they shall go there and kill the Pesach (lamb) in the evening, at sunset, at the third part of the day.

They shall offer its blood on the threshold of the mizbe'ach, and shall place its fat on the fire, which is on the mizbe'ach, and they shall eat its flesh roasted with fire in the yard of the house, which has been sanctified in the name of Yahuah.
They may not celebrate the Pesach in their cities, nor in any place except at the tabernacle of Yahuah, or before His house where His name has dwelt. They shall not stray from Yahuah.

Mosheh, command the children of Yasharal to observe the ordinances of the Pesach, as it was commanded to you. Declare to them every year the purpose and time of the festival of unleavened bread. They should eat unleavened bread seven days. They should observe its festival and bring an offering every day during those seven days of joy before Yahuah on the mizbe'ach of your Aluahim.
Celebrate this festival with haste as when you went out from Mitsrayim and you entered into the wilderness of Shur, because on the shore of the sea you completed it (the exodus). [
I made this law known to you the days of the Shabbaths in the desert of Sinai, between Elim and Sinai.

I told you of the Shabbaths of the land on Mount Sinai, and I told you of the jubilee years in the Shabbaths of years, but have I not told you the year of it until you enter the land which you are to possess.
Keep the Shabbaths of the land while they live on it, and they shall know the jubilee year.

I have ordained for you the year of weeks and the years and the jubilees. There are forty-nine jubilees from the days of Adawm until this day, and one week and two years, and there are forty years yet to come for learning the mitsvot of Yahuah, until they pass over into the land of Kena'an, crossing the Yarden to the west. S The jubilees shall pass by until Yasharal is cleansed from all guilt of fornication, and uncleanness, and pollution, and sin, and error, and it dwells with confidence in all the land. There shall be no more Satan or any evil one, and the land shall be clean from that time forever.
I have written down the mitsvah for them regarding the Shabbaths and all the mishpatim of its laws for you.
Six days will you labor, but the seventh day is the Shabbath of Yahuah your Aluahim.

You shall do no manner of work in it, you and your sons, and your menservants and your maidservants, and all your cattle and travelers also who lodge with you. The man that does any work on it shall die. Whoever desecrates that day, whoever has sex with his wife, whoever says he will do something on it, or he that will set out on a journey on it in regard to any buying or selling, or whoever draws water on it which he had not prepared for himself on the sixth day, and whoever takes up any burden to carry it out of his tent or out of his house shall die.

You shall do no work whatsoever on the Shabbath day except what you have prepared for yourselves on the sixth day, so as to eat, and drink, and rest. Keep Shabbath free from all work on that day. It is to bless Yahuah your Aluahim, who has given you a day of festival and a holy day, and a day of the holy kingdom. This is a day for Yasharal among all their days forever.

Great is the honor which Yahuah has given to Yasharal that they should eat, drink, and be satisfied on this festival day. Rest on it from all labor, which belongs to the labor of the children of men, except burning frankincense and bringing offerings and sacrifices before Yahuah for days and for Shabbaths.

Only this work shall be done on the Shabbath days in the sanctuary of Yahuah your Aluahim so that they may atone for Yasharal with sacrifice continually from day to day for a memorial pleasing before Yahuah, so that He may always receive them from day to day according to what you have been commanded.

Every man who does any work on it, or takes a trip, or tills his farm, whether in his house or any other place, and whoever lights a fire, or rides a beast, or travels by ship on the sea shall die. And whoever strikes or kills anything, or slaughters a beast or a bird, or whoever catches an animal or a bird or a fish, or whoever fasts or makes war on the Shabbaths, the man who does any of these things on the Shabbath shall die. This is done so that the children of Yasharal will observe the Shabbaths according to the mitsvot regarding the Shabbaths of the land. It is written in the tablets, which He gave into my hands that I should write out for you the laws of the seasons, and the seasons according to the division of their days. This completes the account of the division of the days.
